*Haryana has two Real Estate Regulatory Authorities i.e. 1 for Gurugram and another at Panchkula for Rest of Haryana.
Summary:
 All States/UTs have notified Rules under RERA except Nagaland, which is under process to notify the Rules.
 32 States/UTs have set up Real Estate Regulatory Authority (Regular - 27, Interim – 05). Ladakh, Meghalaya, Nagaland and Sikkim are yet to establish
Real Estate Regulatory Authority.
 28 States/UTs have set up Real Estate Appellate Tribunal (Regular -24, Interim – 04). Arunachal Pradesh, Jammu & Kashmir, Ladakh, Meghalaya,
Mizoram, Nagaland, Sikkim and West Bengal are yet to establish Appellate Tribunal.
 Real Estate Regulatory Authorities of 30 States/UTs have operationalized their Websites under the provisions of RERA. Arunachal Pradesh and
Manipur are yet to operationalize.
 26 States/UTs have appointed Adjudicating Officer. 10 States/UTs i.e., Arunachal Pradesh, Bihar, Manipur, Meghalaya, Nagaland, Sikkim, Uttarakhand,
West Bengal, Jammu & Kashmir, Ladakh are yet to appoint Adjudicating Officer.
 1,42,420 Real Estate Projects and 97,885 Real Estate Agents have registered under RERA across the Country.
 1,40,144 Complaints have been disposed-off by the Real Estate Regulatory Authorities/ Adjudicating Officer across the Country.
20.03.2025 378
CONSTRUCTION OF PIPELINES BY IO, BPCL AND HPCL
3300. SHRI RAVINDRA VASANTRAO CHAVAN:
SHRI SUDHEER GUPTA:
SHRI DHAIRYASHEEL SAMBHAJIRAO MANE:
Will the Minister of PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS be pleased to
state:
(a) whether India's largest refineries, Indian Oil, Bharat Petroleum Corporation
Ltd., and Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Ltd., have jointly constructed the
world's longest 2800 km pipeline from Kandla, Gujarat to Gorakhpur, Uttar
Pradesh and if so, the details thereof;
(b) the total expenditure incurred on the construction of the said pipeline;
(c) whether the said pipeline has been fully operational and commissioned;
(d) if so, the details thereof and if not, the time by which it is likely to be fully
operational; and
(e) the extent to which the said pipeline network would reduce the transportation
cost of fuel along with the reduction of the burden of fuel cost on the common
public?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PETROLEUM AND
NATURAL GAS; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TOURISM
(SHRI SURESH GOPI):
(a) to (e): Kandla Gorakhpur LPG Pipeline (KGPL) project is being implemented
by IHB Ltd, a Joint Venture (JV) of Indian Oil Corporation Ltd. (IOCL), Hindustan
Petroleum Corporation Ltd. (HPCL) and Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
(BPCL). The project is progressing and physical progress as of now is 90% and
20.03.2025 379
financial progress is 71%. KGPL project consists of a 2805 km long LPG pipeline
at a total cost of ₹10,088 Crore. Total expenditure incurred so far on construction
of KGPL is ₹ 7,148 Crore. PNGRB has authorized completion of the said pipeline
by 31st March 2025. Commissioning of this pipeline will lead to estimated
reduction in running of bulk Tank Trucks by 185 Million KMs, along with
estimated reduction in HSD consumption by 59,000 KL.
BREAKDOWN OF HOUSEHOLD WITH AND WITHOUT ELECTRICITY
3301. SHRI GURMEET SINGH MEET HAYER:
Will the Minister of POWER be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the total number of households with and without electricity
access across the country, district-wise;
(b) the details of the number of households have and have not 24x7 ensured
electricity supply; and
(c) the details of the areas with 24x7 electricity assurance, instances of power
downtime reported during the last three years along with reasons and
average downtime duration?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF POWER; AND MINISTER
OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF NEW AND RENEWABLE ENERGY (SHRI
SHRIPAD YESSO NAIK):
(a) and (b): Electricity being a concurrent subject, supply and distribution of
electricity to the consumers is within the purview of the respective State
Government/Power Utility. Government of India has been supporting the States/
UTs through schemes like Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Gram Jyoti Yojana
20.03.2025 380
(DDUGJY), Integrated Power Development Scheme (IPDS), Pradhan Mantri
Sahaj Bijli Har Ghar Yojana (SAUBHAGYA) to improve access and quality of
power supply to all consumers. These schemes stand closed as on 31.03.2022.
Under DDUGJY, electrification of all census villages was taken up and a
total of 18,374 villages were electrified (State/UT-wise details are given in the
enclosed Statement-I). Under SAUBHAGYA, as reported by States,
electrification of all willing households was completed by 31st March, 2019. A total
of 2.86 crore households were electrified during SAUBHAGYA period. (State-
wise details are given in the enclosed Statement-II)
Government of India is further supporting the States for grid electrification
of households left-out during SAUBHAGYA, under the ongoing scheme of
Revamped Distribution Sector Scheme (RDSS), launched in July,2021.In
addition, all households belonging to Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group
(PVTG) identified under PM-JANMAN (Pradhan Mantri Janjati Adivasi Nyaya
Maha Abhiyan) and households belonging to Scheduled Tribes under DA-JGUA
(Dharti Aaba Janjatiya Gram Utkarsh Abhiyan) are being sanctioned for on-grid
electricity connection under RDSS as per the scheme guidelines. Till date, works
amounting to Rs. 4,643 Cr. have been sanctioned for electrification of 10,19,030
households (State/UT-wise details are given in the enclosed Statement-III). All
Household electrification works sanctioned under RDSS are to be completed by
the sunset date of the scheme.
(c): As per Rule (10) of the Electricity (Rights of Consumers) Rules, 2020, the
distribution licensee shall supply 24x7 power to all consumers. However, the
20.03.2025 381
Commission may specify lower hours of supply for some categories of
consumers like agriculture. The Rules are applicable for all States and for all
areas including urban and rural areas. The data related to hours of supply in
States/ UTs below feeder level i.e. at household level, is not maintained by
Ministry of Power.
There is adequate availability of power in the country. Present installed
generation capacity in the country is 466 GW. Government of India has
addressed the critical issue of power deficiency by adding 234 GW of generation
capacity since April, 2014 transforming the country from power deficit to power
sufficient. Further, addition of 2,01,088 ckm of Transmission lines, 7,78,017 MVA
of Transformation capacity and 82,790 MW of Inter-Regional transmission
capacity has been achieved since 2014 with capability of transferring 1,18,740
MW from one corner of the country to another. However, there are few States
that have met with shortages in the last three years. The State/UT-wise power
supply position for last three years is given in the enclosed Statement-IV.
Ministry has advised these States to procure power from the surplus
States/Generating Companies (GENCOs). Further, Government of India has
taken following measures to ensure uninterrupted and reliable power supply in
the Country:
i. Steady supply of coal to all the thermal power plants is being ensured to
prevent fuel shortages.
ii. Gas-based power plants of NTPC as well as other generators are being
scheduled during high power demand period.
20.03.2025 382
iii. All the GENCOs including IPPs and Central generating stations have been
advised to generate and maintain full availability on daily basis excluding
the period of planned maintenance or forced outage.
iv. Hydro based generation is being scheduled in a manner so as to conserve
water for meeting demand during peak period.
v. Planned maintenance of generating units is being minimized during period
of high demand.
vi. New power generation capacity is being monitored closely for timely
addition.
vii. Government has facilitated power trading through regulatory framework
whereby states with surplus generation can sell power to states which are
in deficit through three (3) power exchanges viz. Indian Energy Exchange
(IEX), Power Exchange India Ltd (PXIL) and Hindustan Power Exchange
Ltd.
viii. Electricity market has been reformed by adding the Real Time Market
(RTM), Green Day Ahead Market (GDAM), Green Term Ahead Market
(GTAM), High Price Day Ahead Market (HPDAM) in Power exchange.
Also, there is DEEP portal (Discovery of Efficiency Electricity Price) for e-
bidding and e-Reverse for procurement of short-term power by DISCOMs.

URBAN CHALLENGE FUND
3302. SHRI T.M. SELVAGANAPATHI:
Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government proposes to set up an Urban Challenge Fund of
Rupees one lakh crore to implement the proposals for cities as growth hubs,
creative redevelopment of cities and water and sanitation facilities and if so,
the details thereof;
(b) whether the Government also proposes to set up a National Geospatial
Mission to develop foundational geospatial infrastructure and data and if so,
the details thereof;
(c) whether it is a fact that the said mission would facilitate the modernization of
land records, urban planning and the design of infrastructure projects; and
(d) if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a): To give boost to the Urban Infrastructure sector, in the Budget 2025-26,
Government has announced setting up of Urban Challenge Fund of ₹1 lakh crore
to implement the proposals for ‘Cities as Growth Hubs’,‘Creative Redevelopment
of Cities’ and ‘Water and Sanitation’.
This fund finances up to 25 per cent of the cost of bankable projects with
a stipulation that at least 50 per cent of the cost is funded from bonds, bank
loans, and Public Private Partnerships.
20.03.2025 393
(b) to (d): Department of Science & Technology has informed that National
Geospatial Mission has been announced in Union Budget 2025-26 with an outlay
of ₹100 Crores to develop foundational geospatial infrastructure and data. Digital
Elevation Model (DEM) is one of the components for the creation of robust
geospatial infrastructure. This is in alignment with the National Geospatial Policy,
2022 which envisages to develop a high-resolution topographical survey &
mapping system and a high accuracy DEM.
DEPENDENCE ON GLOBAL MARKET CRUDE OIL FOR DOMESTIC USE
3303. SHRI ADITYA YADAV:
Will the Minister of PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS be pleased to
state:
(a) whether the Government has taken cognizance of the fact that the country is
the world’s third-biggest oil importer and consumer and is dependent for
crude oil from various sources in the global market to meet its domestic
demand; and
(b) if so, the details thereof along with the steps proposed to be taken by the
Government keeping in mind that the country’s demand for petroleum
products is likely to increase in the coming years?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PETROLEUM AND
NATURAL GAS; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TOURISM
(SHRI SURESH GOPI):
20.03.2025 394
(a) and (b): India is the third largest oil importer in the world. Energy consumption
in the country has been increasing continuously due to sustained economic
growth over the last few years.
Government is taking various steps to provide for the country’s energy
security, through, inter alia, increasing domestic production of oil and gas,
diversifying import sources to new countries and regions; and diversifying energy
sources beyond traditional hydrocarbons’ to emerging fuels like Ethanol,
Compressed Biogas, Hydrogen etc through schemes such as Ethanol Blending
Program (EBP), Sustainable Alternative Towards Affordable Transportation
(SATAT) etc.
To ensure security of crude supplies and to mitigate the risk of
dependence on crude oil from single region, Indian Oil Public Sector
Undertakings (PSUs) have diversified their crude basket and are procuring crude
from countries located at various geographical locations viz. Middle East, Africa,
North America, South America etc.
Further, the Government has adopted a multi-pronged strategy to reduce
the dependency on crude oil while securing the crude oil supplies for refineries,
which include following:-
i. Increasing domestic production of oil and natural gas through various
policies initiatives, etc. to reduce import dependency.
ii. Promoting usage of natural gas as fuel/feedstock across the country
towards increasing the share of natural gas in economy and moving
towards gas-based economy to reduce dependence on crude oil.
20.03.2025 395
iii. Promotion of renewable and alternate fuels like ethanol, second
generation ethanol, compressed biogas, biodiesel,Green Hydrogen
and EVs, to reduce import of crude oil while also drive clean energy
adoption.
iv. Diversification of crude oil supplies beyond Middle East and OPEC
countries to secure stable supplies to refineries.
v. Increasing refining capacity
vi. Enhancing Strategic Petroleum Reserve capacity to mitigate supply
risks and cushion against price volatility.
vii. Refinery process improvements and promotion of energy efficiency
and conservation to optimise operations.
viii. Exploring investment opportunities in producing/near producing
overseas assets in stable economies.

FLYOVER AT CWRDM-PANATH THAZHAM ROAD IN KOZHIKODE
3305. SHRI M. K. RAGHAVAN:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to
state:
(a) whether the Government proposes to take necessary steps to develop New
Mysore-Kutta-Purakkattiri (Kozhikode) Highway and if so, the details thereof;
and
(b) whether the Government has taken any steps for construction of flyover at
CWRDM-Panath Thazham Road in Kozhikode district under Central Road
and Infrastructure Fund (CRIF) in view of the proposals from Member of
Parliament (MP) and the State Government and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
(a) to (b): No such proposal is under consideration. The Ministry of Road
transport and Highways is primarily responsible for the development and
maintenance of National Highways (NHs) in the country. The CWRDM-Panath
Thazham road is not a NH. The proposal for construction of flyover at CWRDM-
20.03.2025 400
Panath Thazham Road in Kozhikode district has not been received from State
Government under Central Road and Infrastructure Fund (CRIF) scheme.
DHUBRI-PHULBARI BRIDGE PROJECT
3306. MD. RAKIBUL HUSSAIN:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to
state:
(a) the current status and the projected timeline for the completion of the Dhubri-
Phulbari Bridge project; and
(b) whether the Government can provide the timeline and roadmap for the
completion of the three thousand kilometers of new National Highways
proposed by Assam Public Works Department, if so, the details thereof and if
not, the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
(a): As on 10.03.2025, the physical progress of the Dhubri-Phulbari Bridge
Project is 58.57% and scheduled date of completion of the Project is 12.09.2028.
(b): Works on notified National Highways (NHs), including capacity
augmentation under various schemes, are undertaken based on traffic density,
road conditions, inter-se priority and synergy with PM Gati Shakti National Master
Plan. A total of 1867 Km of National Highways is under the jurisdiction of Assam
Public Works Department (NH Works). Currently, 14 NH projects of Assam PWD
covering a length of 133 km, with an estimated cost of Rs. 2,129 crore, are in
20.03.2025 401
progress in Assam. Of these, 15 km have been completed. The projects are at
different stages of construction and are expected to be completed in phases by
the Financial year 2027-28.

2 7 9
OIL AND NATURAL GAS DEPOSITS IN MANIPUR
3309. DR. ANGOMCHA BIMOL AKOIJAM:
Will the MINISTER OF PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS be pleased to
state:
(a) whether any survey has been conducted by the Government to identify oil and
natural gas deposits in Manipur by the Directorate General of Hydrocarbons
(DGH) or any other agency, if so, the details of such findings and the
Government assessment of these deposits;
(b) whether there is any plan or if any permission has been granted to explore,
drill and extract oil and natural gas from Manipur;
(c) if so, the details thereof, including the locations and the terms of such
permissions, if any; and
(d) whether any arrangements have been made to benefit local people or the
State Government from such endeavours and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PETROLEUM AND
NATURAL GAS; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TOURISM
(SHRI SURESH GOPI):
20.03.2025 426
(a) to (d): Government had awarded two blocks AA-ONN-2009/1 (admeasuring
area 2217 Sq. Km.) and AA-ONN-2009/2 (admeasuring area 1740 Sq. Km.) under
New Exploration Licensing Policy (NELP) falling in the districts of Jiribam,
Tamenglong & Churachandpur in 2010. Thereafter, Petroleum Exploration
License (PEL) was granted by Government of Manipur for the blocks AA-ONN-
2009/1 and AA-ONN-2009/2 on 23.09.2010 and 20.09.2010 respectively for a
period of 04 years. The details of survey carried out in these blocks are as follows:

Government has conducted Airborne Gravity Gradiometry & Gravity

(FLKM) 2D Seismic data has been acquired in Manipur. The data is available in
National Data Repository (NDR).

the operator.

MONETISATION OF HIGHWAYS THROUGH TOT
3311. SHRI SUDAMA PRASAD:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to
state:
(a) whether the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has raised Rs. 8353
crore through monetisation of highways through Toll Operate Transfer (ToT)
and if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether the Government concede the slowdown in highway construction due
to static project awarding during the Financial Year (FY) 2025-26 and if so,
the reasons therefor;
(c) the total length of highways awarded for construction during the last five fiscal
years along with a comparison of completion rates, year-wise;
(d) the details of funds raised by NHAI through highway monetization, particularly
under the ToT mode during FY 2019-24;
(e) the manner in which the funds have been utilized and the details of the specific
projects thereof; and
(f) whether the Government has conducted any Environmental Impact
Assessment (EIA) of the awarded highway projects and if so, the details of
the report thereof?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
20.03.2025 430
(a): National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has raised the fund of Rs.
6,661 crore through TOT Mode for the financial year 2024-25 (as on date). In
addition, the bids for the projects under TOT-15 are under evaluation.
(b) and (c): The details of National Highways (NHs) awarded and constructed
during the last five fiscal years are as follows:
Awards/Construction 2019-20 2020-21 2021-22 2022-23 2023-24
Awards(km) 8,948 10,964 12,731 12,376 8,581
Construction(km) 10,237 13,327 10,457 10,331 12,349
(d): The revenue earned through Asset Monetization Mode from Financial Year
2019-2020 to the Financial Year 2023-2024 is as under:
Mode Revenue (Rs. in Cr.)
TOT 42334
InvIT 25900
Securitization 45800
Total 114034
(e): The fund raised through TOT mode is transferred into the Consolidated
Fund of India (CFI), which is received through plougback under budgetary
support.
The fund received from InvIT mode is used for repayment of debts. Fund
through securitization has been raised only in Delhi Mumbai Expressways
Development Limited (DMEDL) and the raised fund has been utilized for the
project purpose.
(f): Preparation of Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) is an integral part of
Detailed Project Report (DPR) for all National Highway projects after conducting
20.03.2025 431
survey, investigation, primary and secondary data etc including consultation of
concerned authorities in and around the project corridor.
WORLD FOOD INDIA EVENT
3312. SHRI Y. S. AVINASH REDDY:
Will the Minister of FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES be pleased to
state:
(a) the details of the total amount spent on the proposals received by the
Government during the World Food India 2023 event held at Bharat
Mandapam;
(b) the various steps taken/being taken by the Government to provide boost to
the food processing industries;
(c) whether the Government has any plan to introduce any Production Linked
Incentive (PLI) scheme in the food processing industry sector in the country;
and
(d) if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FOOD PROCESSING
INDUSTRIES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS
(SHRI RAVNEET SINGH):
(a): Ministry of Food Processing Industries (MoFPI) organized World Food India-
2023 during 3rd to 5th November,2023 at Bharat Mandapam, Pragati Maidan,
New Delhi. Wide spectrum of domestic and overseas stakeholders participated
in the Event, which included 1208 exhibitors, 14 international delegations
including 7 Ministerial delegations, 715 overseas and 218 domestic buyers, 97
20.03.2025 432
corporate leaders, 10 Union Ministries/ Departments, 6 Commodity Boards apart
from 25 States / UTs. Netherlands was the Partner Country and Japan was the
Focus Country of the Event. The total amount of expenditure on the World Food
India 2023 was Rs. 48.18 Crore and total revenue generated during the event
was Rs. 25.96 Crore.
(b) to (d): In order to provide boost to the Food Processing Industries, Ministry
promotes investment in Food Processing Sector through its schemes (i) Pradhan
Mantri Kisan SAMPADA Yojana (PMKSY), (ii) Production Linked Incentive
Scheme for Food Processing Industry (PLISFPI) and (iii) PM Formalization of
Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME).
Under component schemes of PMKSY, the Ministry provides mostly credit
linked financial assistance (capital subsidy) in the form of grants-in-aid to the
entrepreneurs including individual entrepreneurs.
The Ministry also provides financial, technical and business support for
setting up/ upgradation of individual micro food processing enterprises through
credit linked subsidy under PMFME scheme.
Ministry is implementing the Central Sector Scheme- “Production Linked
Incentive Scheme for Food Processing Industry (PLISFPI)” to support creation
of global food manufacturing champions commensurate with India’s natural
resource endowment and support Indian brands of food products in the
international markets with an outlay of ₹ 10,900 crores, to be implemented from
2021-22 to 2026-27.
20.03.2025 433
CANCELLATIONS OF FLIGHTS
3313. SHRI K. RADHAKRISHNAN
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) the number of flight cancellations and the total scheduled flights under the
RCS-UDAN scheme during the last three years, year and State-wise;
(b) the reasons for flight cancellations since 2022, along with the percentage of
flights cancelled due to each reason, including operational issues, year and
State-wise; and
(c) the different types of aircraft affected by cancellations and the percentage of
cancellations for each aircraft type, including small carriers?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
(a) to (c): As on date, under the Regional Connectivity Scheme - Ude Desh ka
Aam Nagrik (RCS-UDAN), 625 routes have been operationalised. 84 RCS routes
have discontinued before completion of 3 years tenure during the last three years
(January, 2022 to December, 2024). Eligible routes which were discontinued in
the past rounds due to various reasons have been re-bid, awarded and
operationalized in the subsequent rounds. Several factors may account for the
unoperated flights/discontinuation of routes. Key reasons for the route
cancellations include disruptions caused by the COVID-19 pandemic, the
grounding of numerous aircraft due to global engine issues, which significantly
reduced the available fleet for all airline operators in the country, the shutdown
20.03.2025 434
of certain airlines and low passenger demand on specific routes. Additionally,
issues such as slot unavailability, low passenger demand, limited aircraft
availability and various internal challenges prevented the airlines from
scheduling the awarded weekly frequencies. Poor weather conditions and
various operational constraints also contributed to the cancellation of flights.
Such reasons would impact any type of aircraft operations on any route. Under
the RCS-UDAN, a variety of aircraft such as Tecnam P2010, Twin Otter DHC
400, Q400, Embraer 175 & 145, ATR-42, ATR-72, A320 & Cessna grand etc.
are operating by various airlines. Aircraft wise data of cancellation is not being
maintained.
COMPLETION OF HALWARA INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT
3314. SHRI AMRINDER SINGH RAJA WARRING:
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) whether the Government has framed an estimated timeline for the completion
of the Halwara International Airport project and if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether the completion of the Halwara International Airport would take place
as per the deadline/target set for the same; and
(c) if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
(a) to (c): At Air Force Station Halwara, a joint venture between the Airports
Authority of India (AAI) and the State Government of Punjab has been formed to
20.03.2025 435
develop a new Civil Enclave. The Government of Punjab (GoP) through the
Public Works Department (PWD) has undertaken the work of construction of the
Interim Terminal Building and allied works at Halwara Airport.
The timeline for completion of the airport projects depends upon many
factors such as land acquisition, availability of mandatory clearances, financial
closure etc.
EXPANSION OF AIR CONNECTIVITY
3315. SHRIMATI MAHIMA KUMARI MEWAR:
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) whether it is a fact that air connectivity in the country has improved
considerably with setting up of new airports and the regional air connectivity
scheme and if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether the Government has conducted any impact assessment of enhanced
air connectivity under UDAN scheme on the economic and tourism and if so,
the details and the key findings thereof;
(c) the steps being taken by the Government to further strengthen regional air
connectivity across the country, particularly in Rajasthan;
(d) whether the Government proposes to enhance financial incentives and
infrastructure support for airlines operating under the UDAN scheme; and
(e) if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
20.03.2025 436
(a): Under the Regional Connectivity Scheme -Ude Desh Ka Aam Nagrik (RCS-
UDAN), 625 RCS routes (including 53 Tourism RCS routes) connecting 88
unserved and underserved airports (including 13 heliports & 02 water
aerodromes) have been operationalised.Total domestic passengers travelled
through RCS-UDAN are more than 149 lakhs connecting on over 2.97 Lakh RCS
flights till date. These developments mark significant milestones in enhancing
regional connectivity.;
(b): The following studies have been conducted on the RCS-UDAN Scheme that
also cover air travel affordability:;
1. The report "The Regional Connectivity Scheme UDAN - Progress and
Prospects" by the Indian School of Business (ISB), (2021-22) ;
2. The mid-term appraisal for the Schemes "Evaluation Study of Regional
Connectivity Scheme" by DMEO NITI Aayog (2023-24) ;
UDAN has been successful in enhancing regional and remote connectivity.
UDAN has provided an impetus to economic development of remote regions and
contributed to a more equitable and inclusive growth in economic activity across
the country. It has provided a boost to businesses with enhanced connectivity to
industrial hubs, tourist destinations and made trade more efficient.
(c): Under the UDAN scheme, Airports in Rajasthan namely, Bikaner,
Kishangarh and Jaisalmer have been operationalised. In addition,
Sriganganagar Airport has also been identified for development and to operate
RCS flights in the state of Rajasthan.
20.03.2025 437
(d) and (e): Promoting affordability of regional air connectivity is envisioned under
UDAN by supporting Selected Airline Operators (SAOs) through concessions by
the Central Government, State Governments/UTs and airport operators to
reduce the cost of operations on regional routes and providing financial (Viability
Gap Funding or VGF) support to meet the gap. The airfare on the RCS seats on
which VGF is provided to the Selected Airline Operators (SAOs) for a period of
3-years, is capped at a subsidised rate, and indexed at every quarter.
PROGRESS OF PMKSY IN TAMIL NADU
3316. SHRI MALAIYARASAN D.:
Will the Minister of FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES be pleased to
state:
(a) the details of the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Sampada Yojana (PMKSY) along
with its progress in Tamil Nadu;
(b) the details of the funds allocated and utilized under the PMKSY in Tamil
Nadu during the last three financial years and the current year;
(c) the number of food processing units, cold storage facilities and other
related infrastructure set up in Tamil Nadu under this scheme;
(d) the steps taken by the Government to ensure effective implementation of
PMKSY in Tamil Nadu, particularly in rural and backward areas; and
(e) the manner in which the Government plans to monitor and assess the
impact of the PMKSY in improving the income of farmers and reducing
post-harvest losses in Tamil Nadu?
20.03.2025 438
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FOOD PROCESSING
INDUSTRIES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS
(SHRI RAVNEET SINGH):
(a) to (c): For development of the Food Processing sector in the country
including Tamil Nadu, the Ministry of Food Processing Industries (MoFPI) is
implementing the Pradhan Mantri Kisan SAMPADA Yojana (PMKSY), as a
Central Sector scheme. Under PMKSY financial assistance is provided to
entrepreneurs for setting up food processing industries with efficient supply chain
management from farm gate to retail outlet thereby giving a big boost to the
growth of food processing sector in the country with total outlay of Rs. 5520 Crore
for 15th Finance Commission Cycle. PMKSY is demand driven in nature and
applications from interested entrepreneurs are invited through Expression of
Interest (EoI) from all over India including Tamil Nadu. Funds are not
allocated/sanctioned/released state-wise under any of the component schemes
of PMKSY.
As on 28.02.2025 under different component schemes of PMKSY, 145
projects have been approved in the State of Tamil Nadu with the project cost of
₹ 1922.75 crore and approved grants in aid/subsidy of ₹497.69 crore. So far,
₹313.3 crore has been released to these 145 projects. The details of number of
projects approved and completed in Tamil Nadu under PMKSY during the last
three financial years and current year are given in the enclosed Statement-I and
the details of number of food processing units, cold storage facilities and other
related infrastructure under PMKSY are given in the enclosed Statement-II.
20.03.2025 439
(d) and (e): Under the PMKSY scheme project proposals are invited from time to
time across the country including remote or underdeveloped areas by floating
Expression of Interests (EoIs) on the Ministry’s website. The proposals received
are scrutinized for their eligibility and evaluated based on the criteria fixed as per
the extant scheme guidelines. Merit based approvals are given to the eligible
proposals based on availability of funds. Wider publicity is given to the
component schemes under PMKSY by circulation through Press Information
Bureau (PIB) and publishing in leading national and regional newspapers.
Individuals including small farmers are eligible to avail benefits under the
scheme.
Government monitors and evaluates the progress of sanctioned projects
under PMKSY by conducting regular review meetings and site visits to ensure
that the physical progress of the projects is in consonance with the financial
progress of the project. Government also constitutes panel of experts
/consultants for undertaking inspection of projects sanctioned under the
schemes as and when required.

INCREASE IN BUDGET ALLOCATION
3317. SHRI JANARDAN SINGH SIGRIWAL:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased
to state:
20.03.2025 441
(a) whether the Ministry has been provided an increase in its budgetary allocation
for the year 2025-26, if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether the Government has earmarked any specific portion of the budget
for the development of road infrastructure in the country, if so, the details
thereof, State-wise;
(c) whether the Government has planned any new projects for road safety in the
coming fiscal year, if so, the details thereof;
(d) whether the Government has provided in the budget for maintenance and
upgrading of existing roads in the country, if so, the details thereof, State-
wise; and
(e) whether a specific share of the budgetary allocation has been provided for
promoting electric vehicle infrastructure in the country, if so, the details
thereof?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
(a): The proposed Budgetary outlay of the Ministry of Road Transport and
Highways for the Financial Year (FY) 2025-26 is Rs.2,87,333 Crore.
(b): The Government in Ministry of Road Transport & Highways is primarily
responsible for development and maintenance of National Highways (NHs).
Majority portion of the budgetary outlay is generally earmarked for development
and maintenance of NHs, including development and maintenance of State
Roads under Central Road Infrastructure Fund (CRIF).
20.03.2025 442
State/Union Territory (UT) - wise details of funds allocated and expenditure
incurred towards development and maintenance of NHs during the last year and
the current year are given in the eclosed Statement-I.
In addition, State/UT-wise details of accrual/ allocation of funds and the
funds released/expenditure incurred under CRIF towards development and
maintenance of State roads during the last year and the current year are given
in the eclosed Statement-II.
(c): Road safety engineering measures / works on NHs, including rectification
of blackspots, are undertaken mainly as part of scope of development /
maintenance works on NHs or as standalone projects in certain cases. Out of
total 13,795 black spots identified on the NHs upto 2021-22, short-term
rectification measures have been completed on 11,515 black spots and
permanent rectification measures have been completed on 5,036 black spots.
During FY 2025-26, 1,000 black spots have been planned for permanent
rectification.
(d) Development and maintenance of NHs is a continuous process. State/
Union Territory (UT)- wise details of funds allocated and expenditure incurred
towards development and maintenance of NHs during the last year and the
current year are given in the eclosed Statement-I as provided in reply of part (b).
(e) There is no budgetary allocation specific to the promotion of Electric
Vehicle (EV) infrastructure in the Budget.
However, Government, inter-alia, envisions development of Wayside
Amenities (WSAs) at approximate intervals of 40-60 km along NHs. EV Charging
20.03.2025 443
Stations (EVCS) have been provisioned as mandatory facility on these WSAs.
Total 393 WSAs have been awarded so far, out of which 94 are operational. Out
of the 94 operational WSAs, 50 are equipped with Electric Vehicle (EV) Charging
Stations.

IMPACT OF IGST ON MRO
3318. DR. BHOLA SINGH:
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) whether the Government has implemented a uniform 5% IGST rate on aircraft
and aircraft engine parts to boost the Maintenance, Repair and Overhaul
(MRO) industry in the country and if so, the details thereof;
(b) the expected reduction in operational costs for domestic airlines and its
anticipated impact on the growth of the MRO sector in the country;
(c) the projected increase in employment opportunities and Foreign Direct
Investment (FDI) in the MRO sector as per this policy change;
(d) the estimated growth of MRO industry of the country by 2030, including
Government incentives to attract global companies; and
(e) the other policy measures being implemented by the Government to establish
India as a global aviation hub for aircraft maintenance and repair?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
(a): Yes. In a major boost to the domestic Maintenance, Repair and Overhaul
(MRO) industry and to the aviation sector, the Government has announced that
a uniform rate of 5% IGST will apply to imports of Parts components, testing
equipment, tools and tool-kits of aircraft, irrespective of their Harmonized System
of Nomenclature (HSN) classification subject to specified conditions.
20.03.2025 447
(b) to (e): This policy change is a crucial step towards enhancing the
competitiveness of the Indian MRO sector, fostering innovation and efficiency
and creating a robust and efficient aviation sector. The reduction of IGST on
imports of aircraft components and parts to 5% will have a cascading effect
benefiting the MRO industry, create new job opportunities, attract new MRO
players and contribute to the sector's growth.;
The Government has taken various measures to create a conducive
environment for the development of aircraft maintenance and repair which
include:
i. As part of the announcements made in Union Budget 2024-25, the period
for export of goods imported for repairs has been extended from six months
to one year. Also, the time-limit for re-import of goods for repairs under
warranty has been extended from three to five years.
ii. New MRO Guidelines announced on 1st September, 2021 inter alia
abolish royalties and build in transparency and certainty in land allotments
for MROs in AAI airports.
iii. GST on MRO has been reduced from 18% to 5% with full Input Tax Credit
from 1st April, 2020.
iv. Transactions sub-contracted by foreign original equipment
manufacturers (OEMs)/ MRO to domestic MRO are treated as 'exports' with
zero-rated GST from 1st April, 2020
v. Exempted Customs Duty on tools and tool kits
vi. Simplified clearance processing of parts
20.03.2025 448
vii. 100% Foreign Direct Investment permitted via automatic route for MRO.
HOUSING/SHELTERS FOR COLD WEATHER
3319. SHRI GURJEET SINGH AUJLA:
Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to
state:-
(a) whether the Government is aware that approximately 6,55,400 deaths occur
annually in India due to exposure to extreme cold;
(b) if so, the steps taken by the Government to provide adequate housing and
shelter for vulnerable populations during the financial years 2019 to 2024;
(c) the details of the studies conducted to identify the regions most affected by
cold-related fatalities, if any;
(d) whether region-specific housing or shelter initiatives have been developed, if
so, the details thereof;
(e) whether the Union Government has taken any measures in coordination with
the State Governments or NGOs to provide winter clothing, blankets and
heating facilities to individuals living in poverty or make shift shelters;
(f) if so, the progress and outcomes of these initiatives to mitigate the impact of
cold weather on the vulnerable population; and
(g) whether the Government has proposed any special package for Amritsar and
if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
20.03.2025 449
(a): The Government is aware of the challenges of extreme cold conditions,
including their impact on vulnerable populations. The data on deaths due to cold
weather and cold waves is not maintained by the Ministry of Housing and Urban
Affairs.
(b): It is the primary responsibility of Government of the States/ Union Territories
to provide shelters for the urban homeless. However, in order to supplement their
efforts, Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs administered Scheme for Shelter for
Urban Homeless (SUH) under Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana- National Urban
Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NULM), through respective States/UTs. It focused on
providing permanent shelters equipped with basic facilities to the urban
homeless. The mission was in existence till September 2024. Also, MoHUA
issues advisories to select States/UTs to prepare winter action plans and run
temporary Shelters for Urban Homeless with necessary facilities to
accommodate urban homeless during the winter.
(c): No such study has been conducted by MoHUA.
(d): As per the SUH guidelines, systematic third-party surveys are to be
undertaken in cities/towns by the local/municipal bodies to assess accurately the
need for shelters at suitable locations. Shelters may cater to the most vulnerable
groups within the homeless populations such as (a) single women and their
dependent minor children, (b) aged, (c) infirm, (d) disabled, (e) mentally
challenged, etc. An actual break-up would depend on local particularities, and
size of the city, and the total number of shelters. States/ULBs may consider
setting up separate shelters to cater the special needs such as Shelter for Men,
20.03.2025 450
Women, Families, and Special Shelter (for old persons without care, mentally or
physically challenged etc).
SUH guidelines prescribe establishing permanent shelters of concrete or
durable and weather-proof alternate structures, with environmentally friendly
designs, rain water harvesting, solar heating/ lighting facilities etc.
(e) to (g): Under DAY-NULM, this Ministry issues advisory to select States/
UTs regarding winter action plan to accommodate urban homeless before harsh
conditions of winter set in. It includes arrangement of temporary shelters,
conduct of mobilization drives to mobilize homeless persons to the shelters etc.
No specific package has been announced for Amritsar for extreme cold
under DAY-NULM.
STATUS OF BHARATMALA ROAD PROJECT IN KERALA
3320. SHRI KODIKUNNIL SURESH:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to
state:
(a) the current status of the Bharatmala Pariyojana projects in the State of Kerala
including the details of completed ongoing and pending projects;
(b) the total budget allocated for Bharatmala Pariyojana projects in the State of
Kerala along with a break-up of funding for each project;
(c) the key objectives and expected outcomes of these projects particularly in
terms of improving connectivity, reducing traffic congestion and boosting
economic development in the State of Kerala;
20.03.2025 451
(d) the timeline for the completion of major Bharatmala Pariyojana projects in the
State of Kerala including the Palakkad-Kozhikode Greenfield Highway and
Thiruvananthapuram outer ring road;
(e) whether any specific challenges have been identified in the implementation of
the Bharatmala Pariyojana in the State of Kerala and if so, the measures being
taken by the Government to overcome them; and
(f) whether the Kayakulam-Adoor-Punalur road is included in the Bharatmala
Pariyojana projects in the State of Kerala, if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
(a) and (b): The details are given in the enclosed Statement.
(c): Bharatmala Pariyojana envisaged improvement in logistics efficiency and
connectivity in the country including connectivity to tribal, aspirational and LWE
affected districts. Development of high-speed corridors will also reduce the travel
time between key economic centers. Various Industrial Hubs, National Master
Plan (NMP) nodes, Multi Model Logistics Parks (MMLPs), Ports and Airports are
also being provided connectivity through these corridors.
(d): The timelines for the completion of ongoing projects are also given in the
enclosed Statement. The DPR is under progress for Palakkad-Kozhikode
Greenfield Highway and Thiruvananthapuram outer ring road. The Government
keeps on receiving/assessing proposals for declaration/ upgradation/
development of National Highways (NHs). Decisions are taken based on the
20.03.2025 452
fulfillment of the criteria, requirement of connectivity, inter-se priority, outcome of
DPR and synergy with PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan.
(e): The major challenge is limited Right of Way (ROW) acquired in consultation
with Government of Kerala. However, the Government is making every effort to
expedite the projects within the available ROW by conducting regular review
meetings at various levels with all stakeholders including State Governments.
(f): No such proposal has been included in Bharatmala Pariyojana.

LAND EROSION ALONG RIVER GANGA'S BANKS IN WEST BENGAL
3321. SHRIMATI RACHNA BANERJEE:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government is aware about the land erosion along the river
Ganga's banks in Malda, Murshidabad and Hooghly districts including the
stretch of Balagarh-Guptipara in West Bengal and if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether the Government has received any representation from local bodies
and public representative for formulation of a comprehensive Master Plan to
restrain/check the erosion on the river Ganga's banks in Balagarh and
adjacent areas and if so, the details thereof;
20.03.2025 455
(c) whether it is true that a large number of villages have been wiped out from
the map and the people have become homeless due to land erosion at the
said stretch and if so, the details thereof; and
(d) the action taken/proposed to be taken by the Government to formulate a
comprehensive Master Plan to restrain/check land erosion on the river
Ganga's banks in Hooghly including Balagarh-Guptipara stretch?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI (DR. RAJ
BHUSHAN CHOUDHARY):
(a) to (d): Erosion, movement and deposition of sediment in river are natural
regulating functions of a river. Rivers tend to maintain a balance between the silt
load carried & silt load deposited, maintaining a river regime. Different stretches
along Ganga river in Malda, Murshidabad and Hooghly including the stretch of
Balagarh-Guptipara in West Bengal face land erosion in varying degrees.
Flood management and anti-erosion schemes are formulated and
implemented by concerned State Governments as per their priority. The Union
Government supplements the efforts of the States by providing technical
guidance and also promotional financial assistance for management of floods in
critical areas.
Ganga Flood Control Commission (GFCC) had prepared the
Comprehensive Plan of the Main Ganga Stem in 1986 which was further updated
in 2004 including Balagarh and allied Areas of district Hooghly, West
Bengal, and communicated to the States, along with recommendations for the
20.03.2025 456
entire stretches, to be used in the preparation of the Detailed Project Reports for
flood protection and anti-erosion works in the related areas.
After receiving a representation from Hon’ble Member of Parliament (Lok
Sabha) Shrimati Rachna Banerjee, Hooghly Parliamentary Constituency, a team
from the GFCC and Government of West Bengal Officials visited Guptipara-
Balagarah area on 16.01.2025 eroded by the Hooghly River in the presence of
Hon’ble M.P. and District Administration officials to guide Government of West
Bengal for the preparation of a Detailed Project Report.
भवन उपिनयम(cid:332) और भूिम उपयोग (लड(cid:167) यूज) का उ(cid:208)लंघन
3322. (cid:174)ी रामवीर िसहं िबधूड़ीः
(cid:179)या आवासन और शहरी काय(cid:170) म(cid:253)ं ी यह बताने क(cid:236) (cid:179)या करग(cid:164) े िकः
(क) िनगरानी सिमित के िनद(cid:165)श(cid:332) के अनुसार भवन उपिनयम(cid:332) और भूिम उपयोग (ल(cid:167)ड यूज) के
उ(cid:208)लंघन के संबंध म(cid:164) िद(cid:208)ली म(cid:164) सील क(cid:236) गई दुकान(cid:332) क(cid:236) कुल स(cid:180)ं या का (cid:177)े(cid:253)वार (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है;

TOLL PLAZAS
3323. SHRI P. V MIDHUN REDDY:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to
state:
(a) the number of toll plazas in the country, State-wise;
(b) the details of toll plazas constructed in the last five years, State- wise;
(c) the number of toll plazas operated by contractors and concessionaires in
Andhra Pradesh; and
(d) the details of amount collected in each toll Plazas in the last five years, State-
wise?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
(a): The details of state-wise user fee plazas on National Highways are given in
the enclosed Statement-I.
(b): Details of user fee plazas constructed in last five (05) Financial Years (Year-
wise, State-wise) are given in the enclosed Statement-II.
(c): As on 28.02.2025, there are total of 72 User Fee Plazas functional on
National Highways in Andhra Pradesh, out of which, 53 public funded Fee
Plazas are being operated by National Highways Authority of India (NHAI)
through toll-collection contractors (user Fee collecting Agency) and remaining
19 Concessionaire Fee Plazas are being operated by the Concessionaires who
have constructed the National Highway in the state of Andhra Pradesh.
20.03.2025 459
(d): User fee collection (State-wise and Fee plaza-wise) for the last five (05)
Financial Years is given in the enclosed Statement-III.

राज(cid:214)थान म(cid:164) िवमानप(cid:176)न, हले ीपोट(cid:170) और हवाई प(cid:229)(cid:280)यां
3324. (cid:174)ी दामोदर अ(cid:250)वालः
(cid:179)या नागर िवमानन म(cid:253)ं ी यह बताने क(cid:236) कृपा करग(cid:164) े िकः
(क) राज(cid:214)थान म(cid:164) गत तीन वष(cid:334) के दौरान उड़ान योजना के अंतग(cid:170)त िकतने िवमानप(cid:176)न, हेलीपोट(cid:170)
और जल ऐरो(cid:369)ो(cid:204)स संचािलत िकए गए ह(cid:167);
(ख) उ(cid:265) अविध के दौरान राज(cid:214)थान म(cid:164) संचािलत िवमानप(cid:176)न, हेलीपोट(cid:170) और हवाई प(cid:229)(cid:280)य(cid:332) के
िवकास, संचालन और रखरखाव पर (cid:211)यय क(cid:236) गई कुल धनरािश का वष(cid:170)वार और (cid:214)थानवार (cid:202)यौरा
(cid:179)या है;
(ग) (cid:179)या सरकार ने राज(cid:214)थान म(cid:164) िवमानप(cid:176)न, हवाई प(cid:229)(cid:280)य(cid:332) और हेलीपोट(cid:170) पर या(cid:253)ी और काग(cid:333) ह(cid:167)डिलंग
(cid:177)मता बढ़ान े के िलए कोई िवशेष (cid:255)यास िकए ह(cid:167); और
(घ) यिद हां, तो त(cid:194)संबंधी (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है और यिद नह(cid:233), तो इसके (cid:179)या कारण ह(cid:167)?
सहका(cid:229)रता म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी; तथा नागर िवमानन म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:174)ी मुरलीधर
मोहोल):
(क) से (घ): (cid:177)े(cid:253)ीय संपक(cid:170) योजना - उड़े देश का आम नाग(cid:229)रक (आरसीएस-‘उड़ान’) के तहत,
राज(cid:214)थान म(cid:164) बीकानेर, िकशनगढ़ और जैसलमेर हवाईअड्ड(cid:332) को (cid:255)चालनरत िकया गया है।
20.03.2025 517
इसके अित(cid:229)र(cid:265), भारतीय िवमानप(cid:176)न (cid:255)ािधकरण (एएआई) ने राज(cid:214)थान रा(cid:186)य म(cid:164) जोधपुर
और उदयपुर हवाईअड्ड(cid:332) पर नई टिम(cid:170)नल िबि(cid:208)डंग के िनमा(cid:170)ण के िलए काय(cid:170) शु(cid:322) िकया है।
िपछले 3 वष(cid:334) के दौरान राज(cid:214)थान म(cid:164) ‘उड़ान’ के तहत िवकिसत एएआई-(cid:255)चािलत
हवाईअड्ड(cid:332) के (cid:255)चालन और रखरखाव पर कुल (cid:211)यय क(cid:236) गई धनरािश का हवाई अड्डेवार (cid:202)यौरा
संल(cid:181)न िववरण म(cid:164) िदया गया है।
हवाई अड्ड(cid:332) पर अवसंरचना का िव(cid:214)तार और िवकास एक सतत (cid:255)ि(cid:248)या है और यह समय-
समय पर या(cid:253)ी मागँ के पूवा(cid:170)नुमान, िवमान प(cid:229)रचालन क(cid:236) संर(cid:177)ा के िलए प(cid:229)रचालन आव(cid:212)यकताओ ं
और एयरलाइन(cid:332) क(cid:236) माँग के आधार पर क(cid:236) जाती है, जो भूिम क(cid:236) उपल(cid:202)धता, िव(cid:176)ीय (cid:211)यवहाय(cid:170)ता के
साथ-साथ इि(cid:184)छत िवमान प(cid:229)रचालन से संबंिधत अ(cid:198)य सिु वधाओ ंपर िनभ(cid:170)र करता है।

कुल 33.16 10.82 73.21
IMPLEMENTATION OF FOREST RIGHTS ACT, 2006
3325. SHRIMATI KANIMOZHI KARUNANIDHI:
SHRI RAHUL GANDHI:
SHRI RAJA RAM SINGH:
Will the Minister of TRIBAL AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
20.03.2025 518
(a) whether the Government is aware of the status of implementation of the
Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (Recognition of
Forest Rights) Act, 2006 and if so, the details thereof, State/UT-wise;
(b) the details of the number of Individual Forest Rights (IFRs) and Community
Forest Rights (CFRs) titles provided to people belonging to the ST
community and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (OTFDs) since the
implementation of the Forest Rights Act, 2006, State/UT-wise;
(c) whether the Government is also aware that many eligible beneficiaries have
been denied titles in several States and if so, the remedial mechanisms in
place for such beneficiaries to claim their IFR;
(d) the details of IFR claims filed and rejected along with the details of rejected
claims sent for review and re-rejected, State/UT-wise;
(e) the number of applications—individual and collective—received by the
Government for grant of pattas under FRA and the number of applications
accepted, rejected and are pending during the last five years, year and
State/UT-wise;
(f) the action taken by the Government on the forest and other departments
officials who are evicting Adivasis and other traditional forest dwellers in the
name of illegality; and
(g) whether tribals and Other Forest Dwellers (OFDs) have been evicted due to
rejection of claims under the Forest Rights Act and if so, the details of tribals
and OFDs evicted since 2019 , State/UT-wise?
20.03.2025 519
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TRIBAL AFFAIRS (SHRI
DURGA DAS UIKEY):
(a), (b), (d) and (e): As per the Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest
Dwellers (Recognition of Forest Rights) Act, 2006 and Rules made thereunder,
State Governments are responsible for implementation of various provisions of
the Act while the Ministry of Tribal Affairs seeks Monthly Progress Report from the
States/UTs. FRA is being implemented in 20 States and 1 UT. The Act provides
for an institutional framework for the claims process, where claims are first called
and reviewed by the Gram Sabha and then forwarded to the Sub-Divisional Level
Committee. Afterward, the District Level Committee decides the claims. These
committees are constituted by the State Governments / UT administrations.
As reported by States/UTs, cumulatively till 28th February, 2025, a total of
25,03,453 titles have been distributed comprising 23,85,334 individual and
1,18,119 community titles. Similarly, a total of 48,99,903 individual claims filed at
Gram Sabha Level and a total of 18,03,183 claims have been rejected. A
statement showing state wise details of titles distributed (Individual and
Community), claims filed and claims rejected and also pending is given in the
enclosed Statement. The segregated details of titles provided to ST community
and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (OTFDs) are not centrally maintained.
Further as implementation is prerogative of state therefore detail of claims sent for
review and re- rejected is also not centrally maintained. The details of number of
claims (individual and community) received for grant of pattas/titles under FRA,
the number of claims accepted (Title distributed) & rejected during the last five
20.03.2025 520
years, year and State/UT-wise are available at this Ministry’s website- on FRA-
MPR- https://tribal.nic.in/FRA.aspx.
(c) and (f): The Act provides provisions of safeguard against eviction and also
provision of petition by the aggrieved to SDLC and DLC (Section 6(2) and 6(4)).
Further, the State Level Monitoring Committee under the Chair of Chief Secretary
is to meet at least once in three months to monitor the process of recognition,
verification and vesting of forest rights, consider and address the field level
problems.
The Ministry has been exhorting all State Governments to abide by the
provisions contained in FRA and ensure that all eligible claimants are provided
the rights which are due to them. For comprehensive implementation, the Ministry
is also urging states to prepare FRA Atlas to map the potential forest areas which
can be considered under FRA and undertake capacity building of all stakeholders.
Regular meetings are conducted with the State Governments on the matter.
Grievances/representations received in the Ministry regarding violation of FRA are
forwarded to the States/ UTs concerned, to ensure that legitimate claimants are
not denied their forest rights. The data related to action taken by the State
Government is not centrally maintained. Further, to resolve field level coordination
issues, this Ministry and Ministry of Environment, Forest & Climate Change have
issued joint advisories to Chief secretaries of all the states.
(g): This data is not centrally maintained. However, the matter related to eviction
is currently subjudice in Hon'ble Supreme Court vide WP 109/2008.
20.03.2025 521

EMERGENCY CREDIT LINE GUARANTEE SCHEME
3326. SUSHRI S. JOTHIMANI:
Will the Minister of MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM ENTERPRISES be
pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has set any targets or introduced special initiatives
to enhance credit flow to Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs)
under the Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme (ECLGS), if so, the
details thereof; and
(b) the number of MSMEs in the Karur Parliamentary Constituency that have
availed benefits/assistance under ECLGS during the last three years and the
total amount disbursed to them under this scheme, sector-wise breakup
thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND
MEDIUM ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
(a) and (b): The Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme (ECLGS) was
launched in May, 2020 as part of Aatmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyaan to support
eligible MSMEs and business enterprises in meeting their operational liabilities
in view of disruption caused by COVID-19 pandemic. The Scheme was
operational till 31.03.2023.
As informed by National Credit Guarantee Trustee Company Limited, the
details of guarantees and the amount for Karur Parliamentary Constituency
under ECLGS are as given below:

CONSTRUCTION OF BED DAM AT GODDAR AND PALAR RIVERS
3328. SHRI D. M. KATHIR ANAND
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has sanctioned any funds for the protection and
preservation of River Palar, if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons
therefor;
(b) whether the Government has contributed any funds for the construction of
the first bed dam at the confluence of the Goddar and Palar rivers at
Erayangadu village in Vellore for the benefit of farmers in Anaicut and K.V.
Kuppam regions in Vellore Parliamentary Constituency and if so, the details
thereof; and
20.03.2025 527
(c) whether the Government has any plans to support the construction of more
bed dams to restore lost river sand and discharge excess rainwater to dry
portions of the river and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI (DR. RAJ
BHUSHAN CHOUDHARY):
(a): The Government of Tamil Nadu has sanctioned the fund to preserve the
river Palar for the following works in the year 2021-2022, 2022-2023 and 2023-
2024.

(b): The Government of Tamil Nadu has contributed the fund to the construction
of bed dam across Palar River near Eraiyangadu Village in Anicut and K. V.
Kuppam Taluks of Vellore District for Rs. 52.48 Crores. This scheme envisage
bifurcation of the river water proportionately towards Palar and Goddar rivers.
This scheme also envisage to enrich the ground water in surrounding 12 villages
20.03.2025 528
in both sides of the Palar river and Goddar river, to recharge 12 infiltration wells
and to benefit 1379.49 hectares of agricultural lands through 626 irrigation wells.
It envisage to benefit 7642 farmers directly and 75000 persons indirectly.

has planned to construct following check dams, Anicut and barrages across
Palar river.

ROAD PROJECTS
3330. DR. KIRSAN NAMDEO:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to
state:
(a) the details and the number of ongoing National Highways (NHs) projects in
the country, State and location-wise, particularly in Gadchiroli in
Maharashtra;
(b) whether the Government has set any time limit for the construction of the said
projects, if so, the details thereof, State and location-wise;
(c) the quantum of funds sanctioned, disbursed and utilized for the said projects,
State and project-wise; and
(d) the number of new NHs (in kilometers) proposed to be constructed by the
Government in the country, State and NH-wise?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
(a): The State/UT-wise details of National Highways (NHs) projects, including
those in Gadchiroli district of Maharashtra, that have commenced and currently
under construction, excluding those which are provisionally or physically
20.03.2025 532
completed and those which are under consideration for termination/foreclosure,
are given in the enclosed Statement-I.
(b): A time period of 2 to 3 years is generally considered for completion of a
NH project from its commencement(appointed) date.
(c): Allocation of funds for development of NHs are made State/Agency wise
and not project wise. The State / UT - wise details of funds allocated and
expenditure incurred towards development and maintenance of NHs during the
current financial year 2024-25 are given in the enclosed Statement-II.
(d): The State/UT-wise details of targets fixed for construction of NHs and
achievement made during the current financial year 2024-25 (till February, 2025)
are given in the enclosed Statement-III.

VAN DHAN VIKAS KENDRAS IN ANDHRA PRADESH
3332. SHRI PRABHAKAR REDDY VEMIREDDY:
Will the Minister of TRIBAL AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the number of Van Dhan Vikas Kendras (VDVKs) set up for value addition
activities out of the 4,000 sanctioned VDVKs in Andhra Pradesh, district-
wise;
(b) the extent to which TRIFED is providing backward and forward linkages to
tribal artisans for marketing tribal products;
(c) whether any assistance/help has been extended to tribals to sell their
products online;
(d) if so, the details thereof along with the time-period since when such
assistance/help has been provided; and
(e) the total worth (in rupees) of products sold online from Andhra Pradesh
during the last two years and the current year, year-wise?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TRIBAL AFFAIRS (SHRI
DURGA DAS UIKEY):
(a): The district-wise status of VDVKs in Andhra Pradesh is given below:
S.No. District No. of Sanctioned VDVKs
1 ASR 16
2 Eluru 4
3 East Godavari 166
4 West Godavari 15
5 Parvathipuram Manyam 37
20.03.2025 539
6 Srikakulam 106
7 Kurnool 2
8 Nandyal 10
9 Nellore 1
10 Prakasam 1
11 Vizianagaram 32
12 Vishakhapatnam 98
Total 488
(b): Under the scheme of Pradhan Mantri Janjatiye Vikas Mission (PMJVM),
TRIFED undertakes empanelment of tribal artisans, procurement of various tribal
products from them and provides toolkits, machinery and training to facilitate
primary and secondary-level processing, thereby strengthening the backward
linkages. It undertakes retail marketing of tribal products through its TRIBES
India Outlets, E-Commerce platforms & Exhibitions like Aadi Mahotsav, Aadi
Bazaar etc., thereby providing forward linkages to the open market.
(c) and (d): TRIFED facilitates the onboarding of tribal producers and
entrepreneurs onto the Open Network for Digital Commerce (ONDC) and their
tribal products on e-commerce platforms.
(e): Products worth Rs. 16.64 lakhs from Andhra Pradesh were sold online during
last two years and current year (till 31.01.2025).
FREE LPG CONNECTIONS TO WOMEN FROM BPL UNDER PMUY
3333. SHRI VISHNU DATT SHARMA:
Will the Minister of PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS be pleased to
state:
20.03.2025 540
(a) whether the Government is implementing the Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana
(PMUY) to provide free LPG connections to women from Below Poverty Line
(BPL) households, especially in the State of Madhya Pradesh and if so, the
details thereof;
(b) whether the Government has identified and provided LPG connections to all
eligible beneficiaries in the districts of Katni, Panna and the town of
Khajuraho in the Khajuraho Lok Sabha constituency;
(c) if so, the details thereof along with the measures taken by the Government to
ensure the success of the scheme; and
(d) if not, the reasons therefor and the steps taken/being taken to address the
issue?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PETROLEUM AND
NATURAL GAS; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TOURISM
(SHRI SURESH GOPI):
(a) to (d): Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) was launched in May, 2016
with an objective to provide deposit free LPG connection to adult women from
poor households across the country including in the State of Madhya Pradesh.
LPG connection under PMUY is released in the name of adult women of the poor
households, provided no LPG connection exists in the name of any family
member of the household and fulfilling other terms and conditions. Households
belonging to Socio Economic Caste Census (SECC) list or from seven other
categories such as Scheduled Caste(SC) Households, Scheduled Tribe(ST)
Households, Most Backward Classes (MBC), Beneficiaries of PM Awas Yojana
(Gramin), Beneficiaries of Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY), Forest Dwellers,
20.03.2025 541
Resident of islands/ river islands, Tea garden / ex-tea garden workers or poor
households not covered under the above categories by submitting 14 point
declaration are eligible for a PMUY connection. Under Ujjwala 2.0, a special
provision has been made for migrant families who can use a self-declaration
instead of Proof of Address and Ration Card to apply for a PMUY connection.
As on 01.03.2025, there are 10.33 crore PMUY connections across the
country. Details of LPG connections, as on 01.03.2025, under PMUY Scheme in
Katni, Panna and the town of Khajuraho in the Khajuraho Lok Sabha
constituency (which covers Katni, Panna and Chhatarpur district) are given in
the enclosed Statement.
In order to improve access to LPG across the country including in the state
of Madhya Pradesh, various steps have been taken inter alia, including
organizing campaigns for improving awareness about PMUY, organizing
melas/camps to enroll and distribute connections, promotion through Out of
Home (OOH) hoardings, radio jingles, Information, Education and
Communication (IEC) Vans etc., spreading awareness about advantages of
using LPG over other conventional fuels and safe usage of LPG through LPG
Panchayats, enrolment/awareness camps under Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra,
facilitation of consumers and their families for Aadhar enrolment and opening of
bank accounts for getting PMUY connections, simplification of process of getting
LPG connection, online application for PMUY connection at www.pmuy.gov.in,
nearest LPG distributors, Common Service Centres (CSC) etc., option of 5 Kg
Double Bottle Connection (DBC), swap option from 14.2 Kg to 5 Kg, provision
20.03.2025 542
for Migrant Families to avail new connection on Self-Declaration instead of Proof
of Address and Ration Card. Further, OMCs are continuously commissioning
new LPG Distributorships, especially in rural areas. Since the launch of PMUY
scheme, OMCs have commissioned 7959 Distributorships (commissioned
during 01.04.2016 to 31.12.2024) across the country, out of which 7373 (i.e. 93
%) are catering to rural areas. As a result of these efforts, LPG coverage in the
country has improved from 62% in April 2016 to near saturation now.
Since the launch of PMUY till financial year 2022-23, Government has
borne an expenditure of up to Rs. 1,600 per PMUY connection for Security
Deposit (SD) of Cylinder, Pressure Regulator, Suraksha Hose, DGCC booklet
and installation charges. From financial year 2023-24, this expenditure has
increased to Rs. 2,200 per connection for 14.2 Kg Single Bottle Connection/ 5
Kg Double Bottle Connection & Rs. 1,300 per connection for 5 Kg Single Bottle
Connection.
To make LPG more affordable to PMUY consumers and ensure sustained
usage of LPG by them, Government started a targeted subsidy of Rs.200/- per
14.2 kg cylinder for up to 12 refills per annum (and proportionately pro-rated for
5 Kg connections) to the PMUY consumers in May 2022. In October 2023,
Government increased the targeted subsidy to Rs.300 per 14.2 kg cylinder for
up to 12 refills per annum (and proportionately pro-rated for 5 Kg connections).
After a targeted subsidy of Rs. 300/cylinder to PMUY consumers, Government
of India is providing 14.2 Kg LPG cylinders at an effective price of Rs.503 per
20.03.2025 543
cylinder (in Delhi). This is available to more than 10.33 crore Ujjwala
beneficiaries, across the country.
Various independent studies and reports have shown that PMUY scheme
has had a significant positive impact on the lives of rural households, especially
women and families in rural and remote areas. Some key benefits are briefly
explained below:
(i) PMUY had resulted in a shift from traditional cooking methods that involve
burning solid fuels like wood, dung and crop residues. The use of cleaner fuel
lowers indoor air pollution, leading to improved respiratory health, particularly
among women and children who are traditionally more exposed to household
smoke.
(ii) Households in rural areas, especially those in remote locations, often spend
a significant portion of their time and energy in collecting traditional cooking fuels.
LPG has reduced drudgery and the time spent on cooking by women of poor
households. The free time, thus, available with them can be utilized in multiple
spheres for enhanced economic productivity.
(iii) Transition from biomass and traditional fuels to LPG reduces the dependency
on wood and other biomass for cooking purposes, leading to a decrease in
deforestation and environmental degradation. This benefits not only the
households but also contributes to broader environmental conservation efforts.
(iv) With improved cooking facilities, there is a potential positive impact on
nutrition. Families may find it easier to cook a variety of nutritious meals,
contributing to better overall health.
20.03.2025 544
Further, as a result of various steps taken by Government to improve
access and affordability of domestic LPG for PMUY consumers, per capita
consumption of PMUY beneficiaries (in terms of no. of 14.2 kg LPG cylinders
taken per year) has increased from 3.68 (FY 2021-22) to 3.95 in FY 2023-24,
and 4.43 in FY 2024-25 (as on 01.03. 2025).
STATEMENT
Details of LPG connections under PMUY Scheme in Katni, Panna and the
town of Khajuraho in the Khajuraho Lok Sabha constituency
District Number of PMUY Number of total Domestic LPG
Connections (In Connections (In lakh) (including
lakh) PMUY)
Katni 2.00 3.45
Panna 1.60 2.17
Chhatarpur 2.91 4.23
Source: IOCL on industry basis
SOLARIZATION OF LIFT IRRIGATION SYSTEMS IN ODISHA
3334. SHRIMATI SANGEETA KUMARI SINGH DEO:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has undertaken any projects to solarize lift irrigation
systems in water-scarce regions of Odisha, particularly in Balangir and Sonepur;
(b) if so, the details thereof along with the number of lift irrigation points being
solarized and the financial allocation for the same;
(c) whether any measures are being taken by the Government to ensure timely
implementation and maintenance of solar-powered irrigation systems in Odisha,
if so, the details thereof; and
20.03.2025 545
(d) whether the Government has any plan to provide special incentives or
subsidies for farmer cooperatives and SHGs adopting solar-powered irrigation
system, if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI (DR. RAJ
BHUSHAN CHOUDHARY):
(a) and (b): There is no project to solarize lift irrigation systems in Odisha under
any scheme of Ministry of Jal Shakti. However, under Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja
Suraksha evam Utthan Mahabhiyan (PM-KUSUM) Scheme of Ministry of New
and Renewable Energy (MNRE), Odisha has been allocated 16,441 solar
agriculture pumps, out of which 5,666 pumps have been installed including 1,549
in Balangir district and 26 in Subarnapur (Sonepur) districts. For this, total
amount of Rs. 8.29 crore has been released to Odisha under Individual Pump
Solarisation (IPS) component of scheme.
(c): The PM-KUSUM Scheme is being implemented in all States/ UTs, including
Odisha. In the scheme, the responsibility of implementation is of the State
Implementing Agency (SIA). The funds under the scheme are released based
on the progress of installation reported by the SIAs and as per provisions of the
scheme guidelines.
(d): Under PM-KUSUM scheme, MNRE is providing eligible Central Financial
Assistance (CFA)/subsidy for adopting solar- powered irrigation system under
Individual Pump Solarisation component of scheme to individual farmers, cluster
irrigation systems, Water User Associations (WUA), Farmer Producer
20.03.2025 546
Organizations (FPO), Primary Agriculture Credit Societies (PACS) etc. Details of
Central Financial Assistance /subsidy available is as below;
i.CFA of 30% of the benchmark cost issued by MNRE or the prices of the
systems discovered in the tender, whichever is lower is provided.
ii.However, in North Eastern States including Sikkim, Jammu & Kashmir,
Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand, Lakshadweep and A&N Islands,
CFA of 50% of the benchmark cost issued by MNRE or the prices of the
systems discovered in the tender, whichever is lower, is provided.
iii.In addition, the respective state/UT has to provide at least 30% financial
support. Balance cost is to be contributed by beneficiary.

एनएचएआई (cid:300)ारा उ(cid:198)नत यातायात (cid:255)बंधन (cid:255)णाली (एटीएमएस) लगाए जाने का (cid:255)(cid:214)ताव वाली सड़क(cid:332)

(ग): सड़क दघु (cid:170)टनाए ँ िविवध-कारण वाली घटनाए ँ ह(cid:167) और िविभ(cid:198)न कारक(cid:332) के पर(cid:214)पर (cid:255)भाव का
प(cid:229)रणाम ह(cid:167)। इ(cid:198)ह(cid:164) मोटे तौर पर (i) मानवीय भूल (ii) सड़क क(cid:236) ि(cid:214)थित/पया(cid:170)वरण और (iii) वाहन(cid:332) क(cid:236)
ि(cid:214)थित म(cid:164) वग(cid:234)कृत िकया जा सकता है। एटीएमएस के िह(cid:214)से के (cid:322)प म(cid:164) कै मरे लगाने से यातायात
िनयम(cid:332) के (cid:255)वत(cid:170)न के कारण मानवीय भूल को कम करने म(cid:164) िनि(cid:306)त (cid:322)प से मदद िमलती है। हालािँ क,
इसके बारे म(cid:164) सटीक डेटा उपल(cid:202)ध नह(cid:233) है।
(घ): सड़क प(cid:229)रवहन और राजमाग(cid:170) म(cid:253)ं ालय सभी रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय राजमाग(cid:334) पर चरणब(cid:294) तरीके स ेकै मर ेलगाने
क(cid:236) योजना बना रहा है।

CHARGING TIME FOR ELECTRIC VEHICLES
3336. SHRI ZIA UR REHMAN:
Will the Minister of POWER be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government is cognizant of the fact that the average charging
time for Electric Vehicles (EVs) in India is 1.5 to 2 hours, far exceeding global
benchmarks of 30 minutes to one hour for fast chargers; and
(b) if so, the details of the steps taken/proposed to be taken by the Government
to reduce the charging time for EVs keeping in mind that these delays further
erode consumer confidence in transitioning to EVs?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF POWER; AND MINISTER
OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF NEW AND RENEWABLE ENERGY (SHRI
SHRIPAD YESSO NAIK):
20.03.2025 669
(a) and (b): Charging time for electric vehicles depends on several factors such
as charger type (or capacity), vehicle battery capacity, efficiency of chargers etc.
Therefore, it may vary depending upon change in any of these parameters. The
charging durations of some of the electric vehicles, as compiled by Bureau of
Energy Efficiency, are given in the enclosed Statement.
Under FAME-II scheme, Ministry of Heavy Industries, Government
of India is establishing fast public charging infrastructure on major highways as
well as in cities at fueling stations.
STATEMENT
The charging durations of different electric vehicles, available in public
domain are as follows: -
