SURVEY ON UNDERGROUND WATER QUALITY
3418. SHRI APPALANAIDU KALISETTI:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has conducted any recent nationwide survey on
underground water quality, including assessment parameters and findings
and if so, the details thereof, State and district-wise;
(b) the number of groundwater sources tested and found contaminated with
fluoride, arsenic, nitrate, iron, heavy metals and salinity during the last five
years and the current year, State and district-wise;
(c) the number of people affected by poor quality of groundwater, including those
suffering from waterborne diseases and chronic illnesses, State and district-
wise;
(d) the number of rural and urban households relying on contaminated
groundwater along with the measures taken/being taken to provide safe
alternatives, State and district-wise;
(e) the budgetary allocation released and utilized for groundwater quality
improvement projects during the said period, State and district-wise; and
20.03.2025 907
(f) the number of water treatment and decontamination projects undertaken, their
implementation status and the number of fluoride and arsenic affected
habitations, State and district-wise?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI (DR. RAJ
BHUSHAN CHOUDHARY):
(a) and (b): The Annual Groundwater Quality Report 2024 has been released by
the Central Ground Water Board (CGWB) based on the ground water sampling
and analysis data from 15,259 monitoring locations spread across the country.
The major objective of the report is to study various water quality parameters like
Electrical Conductivity (EC), Fluoride, Arsenic, heavy metals, Nitrate etc. in
groundwater. The report has found the presence of some contaminants beyond
the prescribed limits for human consumption in isolated pockets of some
States/UTs. Apart from that, the report indicates that ground water in the country
is largely suitable for drinking and agricultural purposes. State-wise data on
contamination reported during 2023 and 2019 for some of the important
parameters are given in the enclosed Statement–I and II respectively.
(c): Consumption of drinking water having contaminants like Arsenic, Fluoride,
heavy metals etc. beyond permissible limits over a prolonged period of time is
known to cause several adverse effects on human health like Arsenicosis,
Fluorosis, organ damage, developmental issues in children etc. However, data
on number of people affected by poor ground water quality is not maintained by
this Ministry.
20.03.2025 908
(d): As such, there is no information on rural or urban households relying on
contaminated ground water. Though both water and provision of drinking water
are state subjects, the Union government is making continuous efforts to ensure
safe drinking water is provided to the citizens. Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) – Har
Ghar Jal, being implemented by this Ministry in partnership with states, marks an
important milestone for providing contamination free potable tap water to every
rural household of the country in adequate quantity, of prescribed quality and on
regular and long-term basis. Following measures have been taken under JJM to
facilitate action on water quality aspects at state level :-
i. While allocating the funds to States/ UTs, 10% weightage is given to the
population residing in habitations affected by chemical contaminants.
ii. To facilitate implementation of the Scheme, more than 2000 water quality testing
laboratories have been set up in the country and an online JJM – Water Quality
Management Information System (WQMIS) portal has been developed. Besides
this, five persons, preferably women are identified and trained from every village
for testing the water samples through Field Test Kits (FTKs).
iii. Under JJM, while planning for potable water supply to household through tap
water connection, priority is given to quality-affected habitations. Further, with a
view to provide immediate solution, States/ UTs have been advised to install
community water purification plants (CWPPs) especially in Arsenic and Fluoride
affected habitations to provide potable water to every household as an interim
measure.
20.03.2025 909
In addition to the above, CGWB is successfully constructing Arsenic free
wells in arsenic affected areas using the innovative cement sealing technology
for tapping contamination free aquifers and also providing technical assistance
to state departments in construction of Fluoride safe wells.
(e): The government’s efforts towards handling ground water quality issues are
mainly channeled through the Jal Jeevan Mission. However, under JJM funds
are not separately allocated for dealing with ground water quality improvement.
Out of the allocated amount, up to 2% can be utilized by the states for Water
Quality Monitoring and Surveillance. As per the available information, from 2019-
20 to 2024-25 (till date), an amount of Rs.4.30 lakh Cr has been allocated to
states under JJM and out of this, total amount released is Rs. 3.95 lakh Cr and
expenditure is Rs. 3.77 lakh Cr.
Details of State-wise and year-wise allocation are available in public
domain and can be accessed from the following web link :
https://ejalshakti.gov.in/JJM/JJMReports/Financial/JJMRep_StatewiseAllocatio
nReleaseExpenditure.aspx
(f): D/o Drinking Water and Sanitation has released ‘A Handbook on Drinking
Water Treatment Technologies’ in March 2023 to disseminate information
regarding new technologies available amongst all stakeholders to understand
and implement the new technologies that address local issues and challenges
faced in water-quality affected villages. The States may take up appropriate
numbers of water treatment system of one or a combination of technologies
depending upon techno-economic feasibility. The State-wise details of water
20.03.2025 910
treatment plants geotagged under JJM for various categories of schemes are
given in the enclosed Statement -III. Further, it is to state that due to cumulative
efforts of all stakeholders, it is reported that from August 2019 to March 2025 the
number of Arsenic and Fluoride affected habitations in the country have declined
from 14,020 to 314 and from 7,996 to 251 respectively. These remaining
habitations have been provided clean and safe drinking water through
Community Water Purifier Plants (CWPPs).
State-wise details of affected habitations can be accessed from the
following web link :
https://ejalshakti.gov.in/JJM/JJMReports/qaualityissue/JJMRep_NoOfQualityAff
Habitations_S.aspx

WATER CONSERVATION PROJECTS IN MAHARASHTRA
3421. ADV. GOWAAL KAGADA PADAVI:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
20.03.2025 928
(a) the details of quantum of water from the Sardar Sarovar Dam allocated to
Maharashtra as per the Narmada Water Tribunal Award, as determined by the
Hon’ble Supreme Court of India in 1979;
(b) whether the Government is taking any steps to implement Minor Irrigation
projects in the tehsils of Dhadgaon and Akkalkuwa;
(c) if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor; and
(d) whether the Government has initiated water conservation projects using the
water of the river Narmada in Maharashtra and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI (DR. RAJ
BHUSHAN CHOUDHARY):
(a): As per the Narmada Water Dispute Tribunal (NWDT) Award, 0.25 million
acre feet (308.37 million cubic meter) of water was allocated to the Government
of Maharashtra from the 28 million acre feet of water assessed at Sardar Sarovar
Dam site based on 75% dependable year.
(b) and (c): The details of the minor irrigation work in the tehsils of Dhadgoan
and Akkalkuwa are as under:
Taluka Dhadgaon:
Minor Irrigation Tank
Department No. Storage Capacity Irrigation potential
(MCM) (Ha)
WRD 1 0.88 105
Percolation Tank
Department No. Storage Capacity Irrigation potential
(MCM) (Ha)
WCD 6 0.87 70.35
ZP 3 1.029 218.00
20.03.2025 929
WRD 115 21.398 4490.00
Total 124 23.297 4778.35
Taluka Akkalkuwa:
Medium Project
Department No. Storage Capacity Irrigation potential
(MCM) (Ha)
WRD 1 19.08 3481
Minor Irrigation Tank
Department No. Storage Capacity Irrigation potential
(MCM) (Ha)

1 2.996 516
Total 2 5.786 878
Percolation Tank
Department No. Storage Capacity Irrigation potential
(MCM) (Ha)
WCD 7 1.39 338
ZP 5 0.52 110
WRD 29 7.1657 1494.30
Total 41 9.0757 1942.30
WCD:- Water Conservation Department
ZP:- Zilha Parishad
WRD:- Water Resources Department
(d): The Sardar Sarovar Project is an interstate, multipurpose project involving
Gujarat, Maharashtra, Madhya Pradesh and Rajasthan. According to the NWDT
Award, Maharashtra’s share is 10.89 TMC.
The Government of Maharashtra signed a Memorandum of Understanding
(MoU) with the Government of Gujarat on 7.1.2015. According to MoU:
20.03.2025 930
5 TMC of water will be made available to Government of Maharashtra from the

Ukai Project in Tapi district, Gujarat by Government of Gujarat.
5 TMC of water will be used by the Government of Gujarat from the Narmada

Basin from Maharashtra’s share.
Accordingly, the proposed use of Maharashtra’s 10.89 TMC share is
detailed as follows:
5 TMC (141.46 MCM) will be used by Maharashtra through Lift Irrigation

schemes from Ukai Project to be used in Nandurbar district, Maharashtra.
5.59 TMC (158.29 MCM) of water will be utilized through the Narmada-Tapi

Diversion scheme in the Tapi Basin of Nandurbar district.
The remaining 0.30 TMC (8.49 MCM) will be used for Lift Irrigation schemes and

Drinking Water schemes in the Narmada Basin of Maharashtra.
PROGRESS OF INTERCEPTION AND DIVERSION TREATMENT WORKS
AT ADI GANGA
3422. SHRI JAGANNATH SARKAR:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) the current status of the Interception and Diversion Treatment Works at Adi
Ganga (Tolly’s Nullah) including the percentage of physical progress
achieved so far;
20.03.2025 931
(b) the reasons for the cost escalation from the original estimate of Rs. 307.12
crore to the revised estimate of Rs. 653.67 crore along with the measures
taken/being taken by the Government to control further cost overruns;
(c) whether the project, originally approved in August 2017, has faced delays and
if so, the details thereof along with the key factors contributing to the revised
completion timeline of December 2026;
(d) the cumulative expenditure incurred so far and the reasons for reported
financial underutilization, given the project's significance for pollution control
and water management in Kolkata; and
(e) the steps taken/being taken by the Government to ensure timely completion
and efficient execution of the project to restore the ecological health of Adi
Ganga?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI (DR. RAJ
BHUSHAN CHOUDHARY):
(a) to (e): The Tolly Nullah project was initially sanctioned with an estimated
cost of Rs. 307.12 crores on HAM Model. The bidding process was prolonged
on account of multiple extensions due to various site-specific challenges. Further
despite best efforts, the bidding process was unsuccessful.
Subsequently, at the request of the Government of West Bengal, the
bidding model for the Tolly Nullah project was shifted from the HAM model to the
DBOT model. The bidding process was prolonged on account of multiple
extensions due to poor response. Further the bid process was cancelled due to
20.03.2025 932
various factors including high quoted costs, limited response and the advice of
the World Bank.
The revised DPR for the Pollution Abatement Work for the Rejuvenation
of River Adi Ganga (Tolly Nullah), Kolkata Project was approved in the 46th EC
meeting held on 23rd December 2022, with a total project cost of Rs. 653.67
crores under the externally aided project (EAP) component (World Bank funded).
The project is currently in the tendering stage.

ELECTRONIC MONITORING OF NATIONAL HIGHWAYS
3424. SHRI RAJKUMAR CHAHAR:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to
state:
(a) the number of National Highways that have electronic monitoring devices
installed and their locations, National Highway-wise;
(b) whether the Government is aware that electronic monitoring on most National
Highways is inadequate, leaving large stretches unmonitored, if so, the
details thereof;
(c) the policies and strategies the Government have to reduce accidents on
National Highways, given the inadequate electronic monitoring and high
accident rates;
(d) whether the Government maintains data on accidents, deaths and injuries on
National Highways;
(e) if so, the total number of accidents, deaths and injuries on National Highways
during the last five years, National Highway-wise; and
20.03.2025 936
(f) if not, the manner in which the Government plans to identify high-risk
segments and effectively reduce accidents without National Highway-wise
data on accidents?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
(a): Advanced Traffic Management System (ATMS) has been installed across
325 National Highway projects with total length of around 20,000 km. Details are
given in the enclosed Statement-I.
(b): National Highways with configuration of 4 lanes or more are being covered
with ATMS in a phased manner.
(c): The Government has already introduced various engineering measures for
reducing road accidents, one of these being, the Road Safety Audit, which has
been made mandatory and is to be conducted at three different stages of
projects:
(i) Design Stage
(ii) Construction Stage
(iii) Operation Stage
In accordance with above, the Road Safety Audits are being conducted in
the stretches based on the stage of development and the recommendations of
the audits are invariably incorporated during execution and OandM stage in the
projects.
20.03.2025 937
The accident-prone areas, which are potential black spots are attempted
to be identified through the above audits and the engineering measures are
taken to rectify them accordingly.
Safety appurtenances such as hazard markers, chevron boards,
cautionary boards, information boards, road markings, studs etc., are provided
as per IRC and Government guidelines.
(d) and (e): The number of accidents along with fatalities has been maintained
on eDAR (e-Detailed Accident Report). It is designed to collect, manage, and
analyze road accident data in a real time to identify accident-prone areas, trends
in road safety violations, and the reasons behind crashes. The list of State-wise
total number of road accidents, fatalities, and persons injured on National
Highways is given in the enclosed Statement-II, III and IV respectively.
(f): The data from e-DAR is used to identify black spots on National Highways to
improve road safety.
STATEMENT-I
Details of Advanced Traffic management System installed

TAP WATER CONNECTIONS IN BIHAR UNDER JJM
3425. SHRI SUDHAKAR SINGH:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) the number of tap water connections provided to rural households under the
Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) along with the progress made in this regard in
Bihar, district-wise, particularly in Buxar district;
(b) the total funds allocated and utilized so far under JJM to improve rural
drinking water supply in Bihar;
(c) the current status of tap water installation and drinking water supply in the
rural areas of Bihar and the time by which the Government proposes to
ensure hundred per cent coverage in districts like Buxar and Kaimur;
(d) whether there are any delays or challenges in the implementation of JJM in
Bihar and if so, the details thereof along with the corrective measures
taken/being taken by the Government to ensure timely completion of
projects; and
20.03.2025 981
(e) the steps taken/being taken by the Government to ensure water quality and
long-term sustainability of drinking water supply in Bihar, particularly in the
regions facing groundwater depletion and contamination issues?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS (SHRI V.
SOMANNA):
(a) Since August, 2019, Government of India is implementing Jal Jeevan
Mission (JJM), in partnership with States/ UTs, to make provision of tap water
supply to every rural household of the country. Drinking water is a state subject,
and hence, the responsibility of planning, approval, implementation, operation,
and maintenance of drinking water supply schemes, including those under the
JJM, lies with State/UT Governments. The Government of India supports the
states by providing technical and financial assistance. As reported by the State
Government of Bihar on JJM-IMIS, as on 15.08.2019, only 3.16 lakh (1.89%)
rural households were reported to have tap water connections. Since then, 1.57
crore additional rural households have been provided with tap water
connections. Thus, as on 17.03.2025, out of 1.68 crore rural households, 1.60
crore (95.71%) households are reported to have tap water supply in their homes.
The district-wise details of tap water connections including Buxar district are
given in the enclosed Statement.
(b) Under JJM, the central grant was released to state during 2019-20 and
2020-21. Thereafter, the state vide their letter dated 28.08.2021 had informed
that there was no need of fund under JJM. In addition, the then Hon'ble Minister
20.03.2025 982
of PHE, Government of Bihar vide his letter dated 18.10.2021 also informed that
no scheme is proposed under JJM in the State. The details of fund allocation,
fund drawn and reported fund utilization during the last five years (2019-20,
2020-21, 2021-22, 2022-23 and 2023-24) and current financial year 2024-25 (as
on 17.03.2025) by Bihar are as under:
(Amount in Rs. Crore)
Central
Expenditu
Openi Report
re under
Year
ng Allocati Fund Availab ed
State
Balan on Drawn le Fund utilizati share
ce on
313.1 787.31 417.35 730.51 473.33 150.34

Source: JJM-IMIS *as on 17.03.2025
(c) As on 17.03.2025, the State of Bihar has reported 95.71% households
with tap water connection on JJM-IMIS. Furthermore, as the State Government
has informed that all rural households will be provided with tap water
connections, including those in Buxar and Kaimur, by December, 2025.
(d) The state government of Bihar has reported litigation-challenges in
execution of drinking water supply projects has led to delay in implementation of
the water supply projects in the State. To address this issue, the State
20.03.2025 983
Government is regularly monitoring the scheme to ensure timely completion of
the project.
(e) The State Government has informed that various measures are being
undertaken to long term sustainability of water source in convergence with other
departments. Some of these measures include rejuvenation of water storage
structures like ponds, Ahar-pyne, renovation of wells, construction of soak pits,
etc., construction of check dams, other water storage structures in small rivers/
streams and water storage areas of hilly areas, construction of roof-top rainwater
harvesting structures in buildings, creation of new water sources, intensive tree
planting, and demand side measures such as use of alternative farming methods
like organic farming, sprinkler, drip irrigation etc. to minimize consumption of
groundwater.
The monitoring of water quality of drinking water sources is being done,
through 01-state laboratory, 38-district level laboratories and 75-sub-divisional
laboratories, by testing the water samples from all drinking water sources at
regular intervals and taking remedial action in case contamination is reported.

NATIONAL CIVIL AVIATION POLICY
3426. SHRI MUKESHKUMAR CHANDRAKAANT DALAL:
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) whether the National Civil Aviation Policy (NCAP) was launched in 2016 to
promote the aviation sector and enhance regional air connectivity and if so,
the details thereof;
(b) the key objectives and provisions of NCAP, including its focus on passenger
traffic growth, infrastructure development and cost reduction;
(c) the steps taken by the Government to establish India as a global aviation hub
and promote the National Air Cargo Policy till March 2025 under NCAP;
(d) the achievements and challenges faced in implementing NCAP since its
inception in 2016; and
(e) the future initiatives proposed under NCAP to further strengthen aviation
sector and regional connectivity in the country?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
(a) and (b): The National Civil Aviation Policy (NCAP-2016) was launched to
create an ecosystem, make flying affordable to masses, increase passenger
traffic growth and to provide safe, secure, affordable and sustainable air travel.
The NCAP-16, inter alia seeks to enhance regional connectivity through fiscal
support and infrastructure development.
20.03.2025 987
(c): During the implementation phase of NCAP-2016, India's aviation sector has
experienced significant growth. The share of Indian carriers in international flight
operations has increased from 36% in 2019 to 47% in 2024. Prior to
implementation of NCAP-16 Indian carriers had direct routes to 25 countries in
2014 which has expanded to 35 countries. Additionally, the total number of
aircraft for scheduled operations has risen from 400 in 2014 to 834 as of
December 31, 2024, with major airline orders indicating further expansion. By
maintaining supportive policies for Indian carriers, India has paved the way to
secure strategic international routes essential for the development of Global
aviation hubs.
(d) and (e): The UDAN Scheme envisaged in National Civil Aviation Policy
(NCAP) 2016 provides connectivity to un-served and underserved airports of the
country through revival of existing air strips and airports. Under the Scheme, 625
RCS routes connecting 88 unserved and underserved airports, including 13
heliports and 2 water aerodromes have been operationalized so far. Total
domestic passengers travelled through RCS-UDAN are more than 149 lakhs
connecting 2.97 Lakh RCS flights. Helicopters operations have been promoted
under the UDAN Scheme. Further, to create an enabling ecosystem for safe,
efficient and sustainable Seaplane Operations across the country, revised
Guidelines have also been issued.
The NCAP-2016 eventually seeks to promote regional connectivity,
infrastructure development, cost efficient functionality without compromising on
safety, and efficiency. Further future tariff at all airports to be on Hybrid Till basis.
20.03.2025 988
NCAP-2016, MoCA, seeks to explore the ways to unlock the potential of land
allocated for commercial use at Airport by liberalizing end use restrictions for
existing and future Greenfield and Brownfield airports. NCAP-2016 provides for
future concessions/development of airports to ensure a minimum level and
standard of cargo facility at airport. In addition to this under NCAP-2016,
provision for adequate land for MRO service providers is required in all future
airport/heliport project depending on potential for such MRO service.
The government has recently modified UDAN Scheme to increase
regional connectivity to 120 new destinations and to cater to 4 crore passengers
in the next 10 years will be launched. The Scheme will also support helipads and
smaller airports in hilly, aspirational and north-east region districts.
MOU SIGNED BETWEEN TRIFED AND TEA TRUNK
3427. SHRI BIPLAB KUMAR DEB:
Will the Minister of TRIBAL AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the manner in which TRIFED-Tea Trunk Memorandum of Understanding
(MoU) specifically contributes to expanding the market outreach of tribal
products, especially in Tripura;
(b) the expected economic benefits from the MoU for tribal artisans;
(c) whether TRIFED and Tea Trunk are exploring digital and e-commerce
platforms to maximize the reach of tribal products from Tripura; and
(d) if so, the details thereof?
20.03.2025 989
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TRIBAL AFFAIRS (SHRI
DURGA DAS UIKEY):
(a) to (d) The MoU between TRIFED and Tea Trunk signed in February, 2025
with the objective to expand market access across the country including Tripura
for tribal artisans and to improve their income by integrating tribal products into
Tea Trunk’s retail and online platform. The collaborations aim to enhance
product visibility of tribal products in premium markets, attracting a wider
customer base, while improving branding, packaging and quality standards. The
partnership may also streamline supply chains, reducing costs and maximizing
economic benefits for artisans.

FARAKKA BARRAGE PROJECT
3429. SHRI ISHA KHAN CHOUDHURY:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
20.03.2025 991
(a) the current status of the 0.4 acre land in Ballalpur Mouza of Farakka Block,
Murshidabad district under Maldaha Dakshin Parliamentary Constituency of
West Bengal;
(b) whether the said land has been transferred from Farakka Barrage Project
under the Union Government to West Bengal State Electricity Distribution
Company Limited, State Government of West Bengal for the construction of
a new 33/11 KV sub-station to cater to need of the increasing electricity load;
and
(c) if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI (DR. RAJ
BHUSHAN CHOUDHARY):
(a): The aforesaid land located in Ballalpur Mouza of Farakka Block,
Murshidabad District, West Bengal belongs to Farakka Barrage Project (FBP), a
‘Subordinate Office’ under Department of Water Resources, RD and GR.
(b) and (c): West Bengal Government has requested for transfer of FBP land
measuring 0.4038 acres at Ballalpur to West Bengal State Electricity Distribution
Company Limited (WBSEDCL) for establishment of 33/11 KV Sub station to
meet up the growing need of electricity in Farakka area. Such kind of land
transfers are regulated by extant Government orders on transfer of public land.
REUSE AND RECYCLING OF WASTE
3430. DR. AMAR SINGH:
Will the Minister for HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
20.03.2025 992
(a) whether the Government is cognizant of the fact that Reuse and Recycling
reduce the demand for energy-intensive and environmentally damaging raw
material extraction, enable waste to be valued as a resource and prevent
pollution from waste leaking into the environment; and
(b) if so, the details of the initiatives proposed to be taken by the Government in
this regard?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a) and (b): The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has
notified regulations for Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) for five waste
streams viz. plastic packaging, E-waste, battery waste, waste tyre and used oil,
in order to ensure environmentally sound management of waste and
operationalization of circular economy.
The EPR regulations prescribe mandatory targets for recycling of wastes
and also focus on reuse and use of recycled content depending on the type of
waste stream. Mandatory targets have been prescribed for reuse of rigid plastic
packaging as per EPR Guidelines on plastic packaging. The EPR regulations
on E-waste, battery waste and tyre waste also promote refurbishment for further
use. The implementation of extend producer responsibility regulations will lead
to further development of waste management infrastructure and recycling
industry, reduction in pollution caused by littered and unmanaged waste and
recovery of valuable material through recycling. Thus, leading to environmental
protection and resource conservation.
20.03.2025 993

WATER AVAILABLE AT VARIOUS RESERVOIRS IN KARNATAKA
3433. DR. K. SUDHAKAR:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) the details of total cusecs of water available at various reservoirs in
Karnataka;
(b) whether the available quantum of water is sufficient to meet the impending
summer in Karnataka and if so, the details thereof along with the action
taken/being taken by the Government to ensure the water availability in the
State; and
20.03.2025 996
(c) the steps taken/being taken by the Government to regulate commercial
extraction of groundwater and unrestricted usage of water from reservoirs for
industrial purposes?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI (DR. RAJ
BHUSHAN CHOUDHARY):
(a): The details of water storage available at the four designated reservoirs in
the Cauvery basin in the State of Karnataka (as on 13.03.2025) are given as
below:
Reservoir Live Storage at FRL (in Live storage (as on 13.03.2025)
TMC*) (in TMC)
Karnataka
Krishnaraja 45.051 28.275
Sagar
Kabini 15.667 8.825
Harangi 8.073 3.198
Hemavathy 35.76 18.966
Total 104.551 59.264 (56.68%)
(* TMC: Thousand Million Cubic Feet)
(b): As per the final Award of the Cauvery Water Disputes Tribunal (CWDT),
as modified by the Hon’ble Supreme Court, the ‘Irrigation Season’ has ended on
31.01.2025 for the current water year 2024-25 (water year runs from 1st June to
31st May).
The available quantum of water in the four designated reservoirs in the
Cauvery basin in the State of Karnataka is sufficient to meet its requirement
towards drinking water, industrial and environmental needs during the impending
summer period of 2025.
20.03.2025 997
(c): Water being a state subject, the regulation and management of ground
water is primarily the responsibility of State Governments. In Karnataka State,
the State Government has put in place their own regulatory mechanism for
regulating ground water abstraction cum usage in their respective jurisdiction.
State Ground Water Directorate, Government of Karnataka has informed that the
Karnataka State has enacted a groundwater regulation framework, namely the
Karnataka Groundwater (Regulation and Control of Development and
Management) Act, 2011 and Rules 2012. There is a restriction to drill a new bore
well in the notified taluks.
i. As per section 11 of the Act 2011, prior permission from the appropriate
authority should be taken to drill a new bore well in the notified area.
ii. The registration of Existing Bore wells in notified area is made compulsory
with the Authority.
iii. Registration of drilling rigs is made compulsory to drill bore wells in any part
of the state. The registration certificate is issued in form- 7A by the
Karnataka Groundwater Authority.
iv. Permission /No Objection Certificate should be obtained from the Karnataka
Groundwater Authority for commercial exploration of Groundwater in the
state.
v. To create awareness among public about the judicious usage of
Groundwater, awareness programs are being conducted for Public and
students in Schools/ Colleges.
20.03.2025 998
vi. The awareness about small children falling into abandoned and failed bore
wells and to avoid such fatal accidents, hoardings and flex boards are
installed at places where public often visit and by distributing groundwater
pamphlets.
vii. Any violator of the provisions of act and Rules are liable for punishment with
fine up to 5000/- and /or six months imprisonment.
viii. Government of Karnataka has passed the amendment Bill called “The
Karnataka Groundwater (Regulation, Control of Development and
Management) (Amendment) Act 2024” to enact a law to prevent bore well
accidents and deaths.
SBM-U IN HYDERABAD
3434. SHRI EATALA RAJENDER:
Will the Minister for HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to
state:
(a) whether the Government has any proposal for allocation of sufficient funds
under the Swachh Bharat Mission-Urban (SBM-U) for the construction of
sewage treatment facilities and subterranean drainage system in Hyderabad
and Telangana; and
(b) if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a) and (b) : Under Swachh Bharat Mission – Urban (SBM-U) 2.0 launched on
1st October, 2021 across all the states including Telangana, a new component
20.03.2025 999
Used Water Management (UWM) is included for waste water management for
Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) with population less than 1 lakh, hence Hyderabad
is not covered under the UWM component of SBM-U 2.0. ULBs having
population above one lakh are covered under Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and
Urban Transformation (AMRUT).
Under AMRUT, 66 projects worth ₹1,663.08 crores having capacity of
18.25 MLD including 4 sewerage/septage management projects worth ₹203.3
crore have been completed in Telangana State. Further, under AMRUT 2.0, 252
projects worth ₹9,584.26 crore having capacity of 1,132 MLD has been approved
by MoHUA in Telangana State. 12 sewerage/septage management projects
worth ₹5,775.11 crore have also been approved.
Under AMRUT 2.0, 03 sewerage/septage management projects of
capacity 972 MLD worth ₹3,849.1 crore have been approved by MoHUA for
Hyderabad.
Under SBM-U 2.0, funds to the tune of ₹463.10 crores have been allocated
under UWM component to the State of Telangana and proposals worth Rs.
934.60 crores having central share of Rs. 463.10 have been approved. Out of
the approved central share Rs. 185.24 crores have been released till date.
DISCOVERY OF NEW POTENTIAL SOURCES OF CRUDE OIL AND
NATURAL GAS
3435: SHRI PARSHOTTAM BHAI RUPALA:
20.03.2025 1000
Will the Minister of PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS be pleased to
state:
(a) the actions taken by the Government to discover new potential sources of
crude oil and natural gas as well as to reopen closed oil wells till date;
(b) whether the Government has implemented any new technologies to locate
these potential resources; if so, details thereof;, and,
(c) whether the Government has identified any new sources of crude oil and
natural gas during the last three years and the current year, if so, the details
thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PETROLEUM AND
NATURAL GAS; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TOURISM
(SHRI SURESH GOPI):
(a) and (b): The Government has undertaken various policy and technological
initiatives to enable National Oil Companies (NOC) and other operators to
discover new potential sources of crude oil and natural gas as well as to reopen
closed oil/gas wells. These, inter-alia, include:
i. Policy for Relaxations, Extensions and Clarifications under PSC regime for
early monetization of hydrocarbon discoveries, 2014.
ii. Discovered Small Field Policy, 2015.
iii. Hydrocarbon Exploration and Licensing Policy (HELP), 2016.
iv. Policy for Extension of PSCs, 2016 and 2017.
v. Policy for early monetization of Coal Bed Methane, 2017.
vi. Setting up of National Data Repository, 2017.
20.03.2025 1001
vii. Appraisal of Un-appraised areas in Sedimentary Basins under National
Seismic Programme, 2017.
viii. Policy to Promote and Incentivize Enhanced Recovery Methods for Oil and
Gas, 2018.
ix. Policy Framework for exploration and exploitation of Unconventional
Hydrocarbons under Existing Production Sharing Contracts (PSCs), Coal
Bed Methane (CBM) Contracts and Nomination Fields, 2018.
x. Natural Gas Marketing Reforms, 2020.
xi. Hydrocarbon Exploration and Licensing Policy (HELP), 2019 facilitating
lower Royalty Rates, zero Revenue Share (till Windfall Gain) and no
drilling commitment in OALP Blocks under Category II and III basins to
attract bidders.
xii. Release of about 1 Million Sq. Km. (SKM) ‘No-Go’ area in offshore which
were blocked for exploration for decades in 2022.
xiii. Government is also spending about Rs.7500 Cr. for acquisition of seismic
data in onland and offshore areas and drilling of stratigraphic wells to make
quality data of Indian Sedimentary Basins available to bidders.
Government has approved acquisition of additional 2D Seismic data of
20,000 LKM in onland and 30,000 LKM in offshore beyond Exclusive
Economic Zone (EEZ) of India.
xiv. New technologies like Airborne Gravity Gradiometry and Gravity Magnetic
Survey, Passive Seismic Tomography (PST), Low- Frequency Passive
20.03.2025 1002
Seismic (LFPS) Surveys have been implemented to acquire high quality
data for processing and interpretation.
The Government has also awarded 144 blocks under eight rounds of Open
Acreages Licensing Policy (OALP) biddings resulting in 13 hydrocarbon
discoveries. Further, Government has approved 20% premium over
Administered Price Mechanism (APM) price for gas production obtained from
new wells or well interventions in Nomination fields which has led to 2280 well
interventions across the country.
(c): During the period 2021-22 to 2024-25 (up to 01.03.2025), total 15 oil and gas
discoveries in the blocks awarded in the production or revenue sharing contract
regime and 24 oil and gas prospects in blocks under nomination regime have
been made by Exploration and Production (EandP) operators.
PROJECTS UNDER NATIONAL GAS GRID IN ANDHRA PRADESH
3436. SHRI B. K. PARTHASARATHI:
Will the Minister of PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS be pleased to
state:
(a) the number of projects that are passing through Andhra Pradesh taken up
under the National Gas Grid;
(b) the progress of these projects during the last five years and the current year,
year-wise;
20.03.2025 1003
(c) the details of the funds disbursed for these projects during the last five years
and the current year, year-wise and project-wise; and
(d) whether the Government has set any timeline to complete these projects, if
so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PETROLEUM AND
NATURAL GAS; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TOURISM
(SHRI SURESH GOPI):
(a) to (d): Laying of natural gas pipelines is carried out by the entities authorized
by Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board (PNGRB). As of 30.09.2024,
approximately 24,945 km of Natural Gas Pipeline (NGPL) network - including
common carrier, spur line, tie-in connectivity, and dedicated pipeline - across the
country is operational and 10,805 km of pipeline is under construction.
The details of the NGPL passing through the state of Andhra Pradesh
taken under National Gas Grid, and the timeline for completion of these projects
are given in the enclosed Statement. The funds required for development of
these projects are infused by authorized entities.

PUBLIC CHARGING STATIONS FOR EVs
3437. DR. KAKOLI GHOSH DASTIDAR:
Will the Minister of POWER be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has issued any guidelines for establishing the
ceiling cost for supply of electricity from public charging stations to the electric
vehicles;
(b) if so, the details thereof; and
(c) the key highlights of any monitoring or evaluation of such guidelines and the
follow-up by the Government to further promote their adoption by the States?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF POWER; AND MINISTER
OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF NEW AND RENEWABLE ENERGY (SHRI
SHRIPAD YESSO NAIK):
(a) and (b): Ministry of Power has issued Guidelines for Installation and
Operation of Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure-2024” in September, 2024
which include following suggestions regarding the ceiling cost for supply of
electricity from public charging station:
20.03.2025 1006
i. Total fee charged by any individual/entity operating the Electric Vehicle
(EV) Charging Station would comprise the electricity supply tariff,
service charge, land cost and applicable GST.
ii. The tariff for supply of electricity to EV Charging Stations to be single-
part tariff and not to exceed “Average Cost of Supply (ACoS)” till 31st
March 2028. The Distribution Licensee may charge 0.7 times the
ACoS during solar hours (9 am to 4 pm) and 1.3 times ACoS during
non-solar hours (remaining hours of the day). As per the data available
with Bureau of Energy Efficiency (BEE), the list of States and UTs
where tariff for supply of electricity to Public EV Charging Stations is
single part not exceeding Average Cost of Supply is given in the
enclosed Statement.
iii. For AC charging a maximum service fee of Rs 3.0 per unit of electricity
during solar hours and Rs 4.0 per unit of electricity during non-solar
hours would be applicable for charging at public and community
charging stations. Similarly, for DC charging a maximum service fee of
Rs 11.0 per unit of electricity during solar hours and Rs 13.0 per unit
of electricity during non-solar hours would be applicable for charging
at public and community charging station.
(c): As per the aforementioned guidelines, BEE has been entrusted to act as
the Central Nodal Agency to monitor the implementation of these guidelines.
Further, States have been advised to designate a State Nodal Agency to
coordinate with DISCOMs and respective State Electricity Regulatory
20.03.2025 1007
Commission for facilitating electricity connection for public, community,
workplace and e-bus depot charging stations. States have also been advised to
constitute a State Level Steering Committee headed by Secretary in-charge of
Energy, comprising of secretaries of Transport, Municipal Administration and
Urban Development, such other members as required to plan and monitor the
implementation of EV Charging Infrastructure at the state level.
STATEMENT
States/ UTs where tariff for supply of electricity to Public EV Charging
Stations is single part not exceeding Average Cost of Supply
Electricity Supply tariff (EV) to PCS

CLEANING OF YAMUNA RIVER
3440. SHRI KALYAN BANERJEE:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
20.03.2025 1011
(a) the details of action taken/proposed to be taken to address the cleaning and
other issues of Yamuna River particularly in National Capital Territory
Region; and
(b) the status of implementation of recommendations in the Report of the
Standing Committee on Water Resources on 'River Bed Management in
Delhi' for controlled dredging to remove debris and heavy metals from the
river bed for revival and cleaning of Yamuna River?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI (DR. RAJ
BHUSHAN CHOUDHARY):
(a): The Government of India (GoI) launched the Namami Gange Programme
(NGP) in 2014-15 for the rejuvenation of river Ganga and its tributaries (including
Yamuna) for five years, up to March 2021 and has been further extended to
March 2026. Under NGP for River Yamuna, 33 projects have been sanctioned
for the creation of a sewerage treatment capacity of 2,130 million litres per day
(MLD) at a cost of ₹ 5,911 crores. The list of projects is given in the enclosed
Statement.
In addition, Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB), regularly monitors
river water quality parameter, inspection of Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs),
and has issued directions dated 12.11.2024 to Delhi Pollution Control Committee
under Section 18(1) (B) of the Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act,
1974 regarding non-compliance status of Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs)
installed in Delhi.
20.03.2025 1012
The Government of National Capital Territory (NCT) of Delhi is working on
the following sewage infrastructure enhancement projects: -
a. Rehabilitation of existing 3 STPs at Kondli Phase II, Rithala Phase I, and
Yamuna Vihar Phase –II;
b. Upgradation and increasing capacity of existing STPs;
c. Construction of New STPs at Sonia Vihar;
d. Interceptor sewer projects.
All the operational STPs of Delhi Jal Board (DJB) are being monitored by
DPCC every month and analysis reports are available on the website of Delhi
Pollution Control Committee (DPCC). DPCC issues communication with the DJB
to meet prescribed standards on a regular basis.
Delhi Jal Board has informed that each contract has provision of penalty
in case of non-achievement of guaranteed parameters of treated effluent, etc.
and, payment is withheld/recovered time to time for non-compliances. In case
the agencies do not respond properly even after repeated communication there
is a provision to blacklist/debar from DJB tendering. DJB has taken action on
defaulting firms at various sites.
(b): The controlled dredging in the river bed is managed by Irrigation and Flood
Control Department, Government of Delhi. In this regard, Delhi Government has
taken expert services of the institution Central Water and Power Research
Station (CWPRS), Pune .
20.03.2025 1013

rejuvenation of river Yamuna:

PRICE OF LPG CYLINDERS
3441. SHRI ANAND BHADAURIA:
Will the Minister of PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS be pleased to
state:
(a) whether the Government has announced to provide cylinders to LPG
consumers at Rs. 500/- per cylinder in various States;
(b) if so, the details thereof;
(c) the details of the consumers who are being provided LPG cylinders at Rs.
500/ per cylinder, State/UT-wise; and
(d) the details where the Government is planning to provide cylinders at the said
rate during 2025-26, State/UT-wise?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PETROLEUM AND
NATURAL GAS; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TOURISM
(SHRI SURESH GOPI):
(a) to (d): India imports about 60% of the domestic LPG consumed. Price of LPG
in the country is linked to its price in the international market. While the average
Saudi CP (international benchmark for LPG pricing) rose by 63% (from US$
385/MT in July 2023 to US$ 629/MT in February 2025), the effective price for
Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) consumers for domestic LPG was
reduced by 44% (from Rs. 903 in August 2023 to Rs. 503 in February 2025).
20.03.2025 1016
The retail selling price of a 14.2 Kg domestic LPG cylinder is currently Rs.
803 (Delhi). After a targeted subsidy of Rs. 300/cylinder to PMUY consumers,
Government of India is providing 14.2 Kg LPG cylinders at an effective price of
Rs.503 per cylinder (Delhi price). This is available to more than 10.33 crore
Ujjwala beneficiaries, across the country. A few State Governments are providing
some additional subsidy on LPG refills and bear the additional cost from their
respective budgets.

MSME CENTRE IN ROURKELA
3443. SHRI BALABHADRA MAJHI:
Will the Minister of MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM ENTERPRISES be
pleased to state:
(a) whether the MSME Centre in Rourkela has not yet been set up even after six
years of approval by the Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs, if so, the
details therof;
(b) whether an amount of Rupess two hundred crore has already been allocated
for this purpose by the Government and about fifteen acres of land has also
been earmarked and if so,the details thereof;
(c) the complete details and the current status of this project;
(d) the steps being taken by the Government to expedite completion of this
project; and
(e) the timeline targeted for its completion?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND
MEDIUM ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
(a): The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA) had approved the
scheme “Establishment of New Technology Centres/ Extension Centres” on
01.11.2018, under which 20 Technology Centres are to be established. Rourkela
20.03.2025 1021
as one of the locations for establishment of Technology Centre under the scheme
was approved on 05.11.2019.
Land for establishment of Technology Centre is to be provided by the State
Government. State Government of Odisha gave advance possession of land
measuring 14.45 acres for Technology Centre Rourkela on 10.06.2022.
As per the direction of CCEA, the Technology Centres are to be
established in Public Private Partnership (PPP) mode, failing which, the
Technology Centres will be established in autonomous mode. Land parcels for
projects, which are necessary to initiate pre-project activities for PPP Projects,
were received from State Governments majorly during the year 2023 and 2024
for 09 other Technology Centres.
Accordingly Detailed Project Reports (DPRs) and bidding documents for
13 Technology Centres, where land was in possession of Ministry of MSME,
have been prepared as per the guidelines for PPP projects issued by Department
of Economic Affairs, Ministry of Finance with the approval of Competent
Authority. Bids have been floated on 11.11.2024 inviting proposals from private
player for establishment of Technology Centre in 13 locations, including
Technology Centre Rourkela.
(b) and (c): As per the approval of CCEA, the budgeted allocation for the scheme
was Rs. 6,000 crores. Technology Centres were to be established at an
estimated cost of Rs.200 crore each. While approving the extension of the
scheme in July 2022, the allocated budget for the scheme was revised to Rs
3,500 crores on basis of expected fund flow from private players for PPP
20.03.2025 1022
projects. This includes revised estimated cost of Rs.125 crores for each
Technology Centre.
The State Government of Odisha has provided 14.45 acre of land for
establishment of Technology Centre at Rourkela.
(d) and (e): The bids for 13 Technology Centres including Technology Centre
Rourkela have been invited on 11.11.2024. In case there is no response from
private player the Technology Centre will be established in autonomous mode.
The construction period for Technology Centre Rourkela is approximately 2
years.

(ख) : ईएमआरएस क(cid:236) (cid:214)थापना के िलए रा(cid:186)य सरकार (cid:300)ारा सभी बाधाओ ंसे म(cid:265)ु 15 एकड़ भूिम

का िनमा(cid:170)ण िकया जा सके तथा खेल और पाठ्येतर गितिविधय(cid:332) के िलए बिु नयादी ढांचे का (cid:255)ावधान
िकया जा सके । 728 ईएमआरएस म(cid:164) से 07 ईएमआरएस के िलए संबंिधत रा(cid:186)य सरकार(cid:332) (cid:300)ारा उपय(cid:265)ु
भूिम उपल(cid:202)ध नह(cid:233) कराई गई है। शेष 721 (cid:214)वीकृत ईएमआरएस क(cid:236) रा(cid:186)य-वार और िजला-वार िनमा(cid:170)ण
ि(cid:214)थित संल(cid:181)न िववरण म(cid:164) दी गई है।
(ग) : 2019 म(cid:164) ईएमआरएस क(cid:164) (cid:254)ीय (cid:177)े(cid:253) योजना श(cid:322)ु होने से पहल,े 288 ईएमआरएस (cid:214)कू ल संिवधान
के अन(cid:184)ु छेद 275(1) के तहत अनुदान का एक घटक थे। 2019 म(cid:164), ईएमआरएस क(cid:236) योजना को
(cid:214)थािपत, (cid:255)बंिधत और काया(cid:170)ि(cid:198)वत करने के िलए जनजातीय काय(cid:170) म(cid:253)ं ालय के तहत एक (cid:214)वाय(cid:176)
िनकाय के (cid:322)प म(cid:164) रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय आिदवासी छा(cid:253) िश(cid:177)ा सिमित (एनईएसटीएस) क(cid:236) (cid:214)थापना क(cid:236) गई। जैसा
िक एनईएसटीएस (cid:300)ारा (cid:229)रपोट(cid:170) िकया गया, ईएमआरएस क(cid:236) क(cid:164) (cid:254)ीय (cid:177)े(cid:253) योजना के तहत वष(cid:170)-वार (cid:211)यय
इस (cid:255)कार है:
वष(cid:170) लाख (cid:322)पये म(cid:164)
2020-21 93,627.50
2021-22 1,32,277.67
2022-23 2,01,494.59

इसके अलावा, एनईएसटीएस ने िव(cid:176)ीय वष(cid:170) 2025-26 म(cid:164) 7338.36 करोड़ (cid:321)पये के आवंटन क(cid:236)
मांग क(cid:236) है।

(घ) : देश भर म(cid:164) सभी ि(cid:248)याशील ईएमआरएस म(cid:164) शै(cid:177)िणक गितिविधयां पहले ही श(cid:322)ु हो चकु (cid:236) ह(cid:167)।
सरकार अ(cid:184)छी तरह से ससु ि(cid:186)जत क(cid:177)ाए,ं िव(cid:178)ान और कं (cid:200)यूटर (cid:255)योगशालाए,ं प(cid:214)ु तकालय और
िडिजटल िश(cid:177)ण सिु वधाए ं (cid:255)दान करके इन (cid:214)कू ल(cid:332) के सचु ा(cid:322) संचालन को सुिनि(cid:306)त करने के िलए
(cid:255)ितब(cid:294) है। छा(cid:253)(cid:332) क(cid:236) आवासीय आव(cid:212)यकताओ ं क(cid:236) पूित(cid:170) के िलए लड़क(cid:332) और लड़िकय(cid:332) के िलए
अलग-अलग छा(cid:253)ावास(cid:332) सिहत आवासीय सिु वधाए ं उपल(cid:202)ध ह(cid:167)। ईएमआरएस सम(cid:250) िवकास के िलए
खेल, पाठ्येतर गितिविधय(cid:332) और (cid:211)यावसाियक (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण पर भी जोर दते े ह।(cid:167) इसके साथ, िश(cid:177)ण
गणु व(cid:176)ा बढ़ाने के िलए एनसीईआरटी, एनआईईपीए और सीबीएसई जैसी सं(cid:214)थाओ ंके सहयोग से
(cid:255)धानाचाय(cid:334), िश(cid:177)क(cid:332) और कम(cid:170)चा(cid:229)रय(cid:332) के िलए िनयिमत (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म आयोिजत िकए जाते ह(cid:167)।
छा(cid:253)(cid:332) के िलए उ(cid:184)च िश(cid:177)ा के अवसर(cid:332) को बेहतर बनाने के िलए जेईई और एनईईटी के िलए कोिचंग
श(cid:322)ु क(cid:236) गई है। उपयोिगता (वै(cid:208)यू) िश(cid:177)ा और क(cid:229)रयर माग(cid:170)दश(cid:170)न काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म छा(cid:253)(cid:332) को सिु व(cid:178) क(cid:229)रयर
िवक(cid:208)प बनाने म(cid:164) सहायता करते ह(cid:167)। सीखने के अवसर(cid:332) को और बढ़ाने के िलए, जेएवाईएके एआर
(जयकार) अंत(cid:229)र(cid:177) िश(cid:177)ा काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म श(cid:322)ु िकया गया है। इस पहल का उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य युवा मानस (िवचार(cid:332)) को
अंत(cid:229)र(cid:177) संबंधी िश(cid:177)ा (cid:255)दान करना, अंत(cid:229)र(cid:177) अ(cid:198)वेषण म(cid:164) उनक(cid:236) (cid:321)िच को बढ़ावा दने ा और अंत(cid:229)र(cid:177)
िव(cid:178)ान और (cid:255)ौ(cid:299)ोिगक(cid:236) के (cid:177)े(cid:253) म(cid:164) भिव(cid:213)य के क(cid:229)रयर के िलए (cid:255)े(cid:229)रत करना है। क(cid:229)रयर माग(cid:170)दश(cid:170)न और
मनोवै(cid:178)ािनक परी(cid:177)ण पहल के िलए तलाश काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म छा(cid:253)(cid:332) को उनक(cid:236) (cid:177)मता, (cid:321)िचय(cid:332) और यो(cid:181)यता
क(cid:236) पहचान करने म(cid:164) मदद करता है, और उ(cid:198)ह(cid:164) उपयु(cid:265) क(cid:229)रयर पथ क(cid:236) ओर माग(cid:170)दश(cid:170)न करता है।
इसके अलावा, सभी ईएमआरएस म(cid:164) शै(cid:177)िणक गितिविधय(cid:332) को स(cid:211)ु यवि(cid:214)थत करने के िलए एक
क(cid:164) (cid:254)ीय (cid:322)प से संरिचत शै(cid:177)िणक कै ल(cid:164)डर लागू िकया गया है। पाठ्य(cid:248)म म(cid:164) एक(cid:322)पता सिु नि(cid:306)त करने
के िलए, मानक(cid:236)कृत पाठ्य(cid:248)म िवतरण (िडलीवरी) और म(cid:208)ू यांकन सिु नि(cid:306)त करने के िलए अिधकतम
(cid:214)कू ल(cid:332) को सीबीएसई बोड(cid:170) से संब(cid:294) िकया गया है।

MSME UNITS IN ASSAM
3445. SHRI PHANI BHUSAN CHOUDHURY:
Will the Minister of MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM ENTERPRISES be
pleased to state:
(a) the number of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) units
registered in the country since the year 2016, State and year-wise;
(b) the number of MSME units currently operational in Assam;
(c) the details of loans sanctioned to MSME units by banks in Assam from the
year 2016-17 to the year 2024-25, Bank-wise;
(d) the details of MSME units assisted by the Government in Assam, district-
wise;
(e) whether the Government has infused equity of Rs. 50,000 crores under the
MSME Self- Reliant India Fund and if so, the State-wise distribution with a
focus on Assam; and
20.03.2025 1035
(f) the status and progress of the Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme
(ECLGS) for MSMEs with specific emphasis on Assam?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND
MEDIUM ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
(a) and (b): Udyam Registration Portal was launched on 01.07.2020. As on
15.03.2025, the total number of MSMEs registered in the country and Assam
State on the Udyam Registration (UR) Portal and Udyam Assist Platform was
6.13 Crore and 10.82 Lakh respectively. State-wise and Year-Wise details are
given in the enclosed Statement-I.
(c): The credit disbursed by the Scheduled Commercial Banks (SCBs) to MSMEs
in the state of Assam from FY 2017 is given in the enclosed Statement-II.
(d): Under Prime Minister’s Employment Generation Programme, the number of
units assisted in the state of Assam during 2021-22, 2022-23 and 2023-24 were
3,855; 2,596 and 2,417 respectively. The district wise details are given in the
enclosed Statement-III.
(e): The Government of India has announced Fund of Funds with the
nomenclature Self Reliant India (SRI) Fund to infuse Rs. 50,000 crore as equity
funding in those MSMEs which have the potential and viability to grow and
become large units. Out of the total fund of Rs. 50,000 Crore, there is a provision
of Rs.10,000 Crore from Government of India and Rs.40,000 Crore through
Private Equity / Venture Capital funds. This initiative is aimed at providing growth
20.03.2025 1036
capital to the deserving and eligible units of MSME sector. This is Central Sector
Scheme, under which funds are not released to the State Government.
As reported by NSIC Venture Capital Fund Limited (NVCFL), as on
28.02.2025, total 60 Daughter Funds have been empanelled with NVCFL (Mother
Fund) and by way of investing more than Rs. 10,979 Cr, 577 potential MSMEs
have been assisted. The State-wise details are is given in the enclosed
Statement-IV.
(f): The Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme (ECLGS) was launched in
May, 2020 as part of Aatmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyaan to support eligible MSMEs
and business enterprises in meeting their operational liabilities in view of
disruption caused by COVID-19 pandemic. The Scheme was operational till
31.03.2023. Beneficiaries’ details of ECLGS are given below.
Total No of Total Amount
State Guarantees Guaranteed (Rs. In
Issued Cr)
Assam 544,917 3,052.27
All India 11,375,919 242,768.69

PROMOTION OF DOMESTIC FOOD PROCESSING MACHINERY
3446. SHRI ARUN NEHRU:
Will the Minister of FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES be pleased to
state:
(a) the details of the measures taken/being taken by the Government to
modernize India's food processing sector and promote domestic food
processing machinery;
(b) whether the Government has proposed to establish a dedicated mechanism
to attract investments and standardize locally produced machinery and if so,
the details thereof;
20.03.2025 1042
(c) whether the Government has any plan to address challenges in raw material
access, regulations and technology transfer for the industry and if so, the
details thereof;
(d) the role of Research and Development (RandD) in enhancing the efficiency
and competitiveness of domestic machinery; and
(e) the initiatives taken/being undertaken by the Government to develop and
enforce standards for locally manufactured food processing equipments?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FOOD PROCESSING
INDUSTRIES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS
(SHRI RAVNEET SINGH):
(a) : In order to promote and ensure overall development of Food Processing
Industries, Ministry of Food Processing Industries (MoFPI) has been
incentivizing setting up/expansion of related infrastructure through its Central
Sector schemes namely Pradhan Mantri Kisan SAMPADA Yojana (PMKSY)
Scheme, Production Linked Incentive Scheme for Food Processing Industry
(PLISFPI) and Centrally Sponsored – Pradhan Mantri Formalization of Micro
Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME) Scheme across the country. These
schemes are not region or state specific but demand driven.
Under Research and Development (RandD) component of the PMKSY
Scheme, MoFPI extends financial assistance as grant-in-aid to Private
organizations/ universities/institutions, Public funded organizations and
recognized RandD laboratories both in public and private sector to promote and
undertake demand driven RandD work in food processing sector for product and
20.03.2025 1043
process development, design and development of equipment, improved
storage, shelf-life, packaging etc.
Under the PMFME Scheme, financial assistance is being provided to
micro food processing enterprises for acquiring new technologies and upgrading
equipment. In addition, handholding support is provided to the beneficiary for
project formulation, execution, access to credit, linkages to machine/equipment
manufacturers, maintenance of hygiene and quality control etc.
(b): MoFPI is incentivizing establishment of such facilities through its schemes of
PMKSY, PMFME and PLIS, which are demand driven in nature and implemented
across the country Apart from this, with the objective to boost investments and
sourcing of Indian food products, MoFPI organized 3rd edition of a Mega Event,
called “World Food India” during 19th to 22nd September 2024 at Bharat
Mandapam, New Delhi to showcase domestic Industry and provide it with
collaborative opportunities with international stakeholders. The event brought
global food processing companies, innovators, supply chain stakeholders,
equipment manufacturers, etc. on a collaborative platform and provide foreign
companies with tie-up/ business opportunities with its Indian counterparts.
(c): In order to promote local industries, One District One Product (ODOP)
approach under PMFME Scheme has been adopted to reap the benefit of scale
in terms of procurement of inputs, availing common services and marketing of
products. It provides the framework for value chain development and alignment
20.03.2025 1044
of support infrastructure. The ODOP are identified by States / UTs based on the
agriculture production, raw material availability, perishability of the product etc.
In order to promote use of technology in Food Processing Sector, MoFPI
extends Grant-in-Aid to educational and research institutions including Council
of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) recognized Research and
Development (RandD) units in private sector for related demand driven RandD
projects through its RandD scheme under PMKSY.
In addition, National Institute of Food Technology, Entrepreneurship and
Management (NIFTEM) Kundli and NIFTEM, Thanjavur, under administrative
control of MoFPI, are also engaged in transfer of technology to industries, farmer
producer organizations, startups, and others. Also, NIFTEM-T undertakes
several consultancy services and industry sponsored research projects. The
need for these projects originate from the industry and the technology developed
by NIFTEM-T will be used by the industry after completion of the project.
(d): Research and development (RandD) plays a crucial role in boosting the
efficiency and competitiveness of domestic machinery. By fostering innovation,
RandD helps improve product quality, reduce manufacturing costs, and enhance
performance. It enables companies to stay ahead of market trends, adopt
advanced technologies, and meet evolving consumer demands effectively. Thus,
Research and Development (RandD) component of the PMKSY Scheme has
been considered by Ministry.
20.03.2025 1045
(e): Schemes of MoFPI are, inter alia, intended to develop value-added products,
bringing innovative technologies, enhancing productivity, enhancing product
quality and safety standards etc.
Based on the demand from the food industry, FPOs, MSMEs, etc,
NIFTEMs design and develop identified machinery for food processing and
transfer the designs to the manufacturers for large scale production and
commercial sale of such machinery for end users.
IMPLEMENTATION OF FRA IN TELANGANA
3447. SHRI VAMSI KRISHNA GADDAM:
Will the Minister of TRIBAL AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the number of applications received for land titles under the Forest Rights Act
(FRA), 2006 during the last five years, State-wise, specifically in Telangana,
along with the status of their processing;
(b) whether the Union Government has received any proposals or requests from
the State Government of Telangana for expediting the resolution of podu land
disputes and land title distribution in Chennur;
(c) if so, the details thereof including the dates of such requests and the action
taken thereon;
(d) the details of the funds allocated and disbursed to Telangana for the
implementation of the Forest Rights Act (FRA) during the last five years; and
20.03.2025 1046
(e) the timeline set by the Government for resolving all pending podu land
disputes in Telangana and the reasons for delays, if any?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TRIBAL AFFAIRS (SHRI
DURGA DAS UIKEY):
(a): As per the Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers
(Recognition of Forest Rights) Act, 2006 and Rules made thereunder, State
Governments are responsible for implementation of various provisions of the Act
while the Ministry of Tribal Affairs seeks Monthly Progress Report from the
States / UTs. FRA is being implemented in 20 States (including Telangana) and
1 UT.
As reported by States/UTs, cumulatively across the country till 28th February,
2025, a total of 51,04,904 claims filed at Gram Sabha Level comprising
48,99,903 individual and 2,05,001 community claims and a total of 25,03,453
titles have been distributed, comprising 23,85,334 individual and 1,18,119
community titles. The state wise details (including Telangana) are given in the
enclosed Statement-I. The details of number of claims filed at Gram Sabha
(individual and community), the number of claims accepted (Titles distributed),
state wise for more than last five years, are available at this Ministry’s website-
on FRA- Monthly Progress Report- https://tribal.nic.in/FRA.aspx.
(b), (c) and (e): Since implementation of the Act is the prerogative of the State
Government, this Ministry has not received any proposal or request from any
State including Telangana for land distribution. With regard to podu land
20.03.2025 1047
disputes, State Government of Telangana has informed that there are no Podu
land disputes.
(d): The details of approved activities along with approved funds for
implementation of Forest Rights Act, 2006 for the State of Telangana under the
scheme of ‘Support to TRIs’ of Ministry of Tribal Affairs are given in the enclosed
Statement-II. Further, Government of India in the current year has launched the
Dharti Aaba Janjatiya Gram Utkarsh Abhiyan (DAJGUA). The Abhiyan envisions
saturation of critical gaps in social infrastructure, health, education, livelihood,
through 25 interventions implemented by 17-line ministries of Govt. of India by
convergence and outreach; and ensures holistic and sustainable development
of tribal areas and communities. One of the interventions is related to
comprehensive implementation of FRA and under this intervention, all States
including Telangana inter alia are to be supported with financial assistance for
setting up of FRA cells and also digitalisation of data and claim process.

STRENGTHENING OF STRATEGIC PETROLEUM RESERVES
3449. SHRIMATI PRATIMA MONDAL:
Will the Minister of PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS be pleased to
state
(a) the manner in which the Government is strengthening Strategic Petroleum
Reserves (SPR) to mitigate the risks from geopolitical uncertainties, global
supply chain disruptions and price volatility;
(b) the steps being taken to diversify crude oil and LNG import sources and the
manner in which the Government plans to negotiate more favourable long-
term contracts with international suppliers; and
(c) the manner in which the Government plans to balance fossil fuel reliance with
renewable energy integration, ensuring a smooth transition to cleaner energy
sources without disrupting economic growth?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PETROLEUM AND
NATURAL GAS; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TOURISM
(SHRI SURESH GOPI):
(a): Government, through a Special Purpose Vehicle called Indian Strategic
Petroleum Reserve Limited (ISPRL), has established Strategic Petroleum
Reserves (SPR) facilities with total capacity of 5.33 Million Metric Tonnes (MMT)
of crude oil at 3 locations namely (i) Vishakhapatnam (1.33 MMT), (ii) Mangaluru
(1.5 MMT) and (iii) Padur (2.5 MMT) capacity.
To further augment the SPR capacity, Government, in July 2021, had also
approved the establishment of two additional commercial-cum-strategic
20.03.2025 1054
petroleum reserve facilities with total storage capacity of 6.5 MMT at Chandikhol
(4 MMT) in Odisha and Padur (2.5 MMT) in Karnataka, on a Public Private
Partnership mode. Government and OMCs evaluate, from time to time, the
possibility of augmentation of storage capacities based on technical and
commercial feasibility. Assessment of new sites for establishing additional
petroleum reserves is a continuous process.
(b): To ensure security of crude supplies and to mitigate the risk of dependence
on crude oil from single region, Indian Oil Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs)
have diversified their crude basket and are procuring crude from countries
located at various geographical locations viz. Middle East, Africa, North America,
South America etc. Further, Government has already diversified the import of
LNG by adding Australia, USA and UAE as sourcing destinations. India has also
signed various long term agreements for procurement of LNG for ensuring
uninterrupted supplies and safeguearding from price volatility.
(c): To counter the reliance on fossil fuels, Government has adopted a multi-
pronged strategy to promote clean energy which, inter alia, include:
 Demand substitution by promoting usage of natural gas as fuel/feedstock
across the country towards increasing the share of natural gas in
economy and moving towards gas based economy.
 Promotion of renewable and alternate fuels like ethanol, second
generation ethanol, compressed bio gas, biodiesel, Green Hydrogen and
Evs.
20.03.2025 1055
 Refinery process improvements, promoting energy efficiency and
conservation,
 Efforts for increasing production of oil and natural gas through various
policies initiatives, etc. For promoting the use of Compressed Bio Gas
(CBG) as automotive fuel, Sustainable Alternative Towards Affordable
Transportation (SATAT) initiative has also been launched.
 To promote the use of biofuels across the country, various programmes,
such as Ethanol Blended Petrol (EBP) Programme, wherein Oil Marketing
Companies (OMCs) sell petrol blended with ethanol, Biodiesel blending
programme wherein biodiesel is blended with diesel, have been taken up.

3450. (cid:174)ी दरोगा (cid:255)साद सरोज:

… (Interruptions)
HON. CHAIRPERSON: When I occupied the Chair, I also made the same request.
…(Interruptions)
SHRI A. RAJA (NILGIRIS): What is wrong with this, Sir?
… (Interruptions)
HON. CHAIRPERSON: What is wrong with this was already told to you, Sir.
… (Interruptions)
HON. CHAIRPERSON: You know it.
… (Interruptions)
HON. CHAIRPERSON: Please understand, we have a discussion.
… (Interruptions)
HON. CHAIRPERSON: We have a discussion on agriculture. It is such an
important topic for the country.
… (Interruptions)
20.03.2025 1062
HON. CHAIRPERSON: On such an important topic like agriculture, you are not
allowing any discussion to take place.
… (Interruptions)
HON. CHAIRPERSON: I am requesting you once again, please cooperate.
… (Interruptions)
HON. CHAIRPERSON: I am requesting you once again, please cooperate and let
the proceedings go on.
… (Interruptions)
माननीय सभापित : सभा क(cid:236) काय(cid:170)वाही श(cid:248)ु वार, िदनांक 21 माच(cid:170), 2025 को (cid:255)ात: 11 बज े तक के
िलए (cid:214)थिगत क(cid:236) जाती ह ै ।
14.01 hrs
The Lok Sabha then adjourned till Eleven of the Clock
on Friday, March 21, 2025/ Phalguna 30, 1946 (Saka).
